user_comment: Perfect Fit These pants are the perfect shade of green and fit fabulously well. I have washed them several times, and they so look as good as new. I’m 5’9, 165 lbs apple shaped. No waist and skinny butt and legs. The waist goes right up to or slightly below my belly button. I do not find them see through but with the white I obviously wear nude or white underpants. I wouldn’t pay full price for these, but am happy with my purchase. If they shrink or do anything weird in the wash I will update. I try to wear jeans a bunch before washing. They are so stretchy that they are extremely comfortable but do not sag during the day!

Very comfortable...but they stink! I think I’m giving these a 3.5, I went back and forth with the star rating. These are very comfortable pants. I wasn’t sure whether to go with the 0 or the 2, and the short or the regular. I’m 5’3.5” and my go-to Jean is a size 1 in Celebrity Pink. I ordered the Size 0 Regular. They seemed a tad long for me (I think I like the shorter length on me in tighter jeans) but they were perfect with rolled cuffs. They are definitely more of a relaxed look than I am used to. The legs were looser. I liked the rise and it was high enough to contain everything but I don’t think it gave me a flat butt the way some high-waist jeans do. Great lighter color. I think I would have preferred the shorter length, but with the looseness of the pants I’m not sure that would’ve looked better. So I’m ultimately glad I went with the 0R. If you are on the smaller side of a size 0, you may find these too big, but that’s what free returns are for. The thing is these pants STINK. They smell so bad. It’s like formaldehyde meets moth balls. I thought after a wash the smell would be gone but nope! These still wreaked after a wash. I’m not going to return them and hope that a few more washes will get the smell out, but if you’re sensitive to that, I’d skip these pants.

I ordered two sizes up and am thrilled with the fit I don't like really tight clothes but want the fitted look. First I ordered my usual size (6 Long) and a size up (8 Long) and found both too tight for my taste. Then I ordered the 10 Long. They are PERFECT. And for us tall girls who have fought the High-Tide look all our lives, the longs truly are long. They hit my ankles, which I love. I started with a white pair and just ordered a blue jean pair the same size. These jeans are currently the best thing going - good price, good quality and good looking. In case it helps, I'm 5'9 and usually wear a 33 inseam.

Satisfaction varies whether you want skin tight or not I like ankle skinny jeans that are not skin tight. In Amazon Essentials Bermuda shorts I wear size 4, generally I wear a six. I do not like my clothes tight. Based on reviews I sized up to an 8. The jeans have lots of stretch and if you want leggings look these pants would be great. For me, they are so so. I did keep them as I wanted some tan jeans and they had been hard to find. I am 5’1” and weigh 100#. My perfect ankle skinny jeans are Calvin Klein Ankle Skinny in size 8, which are a little looser thru the legs and ankle, but I don’t think they make any more. Hope the review helps.

Pleasantly surprised but white has side stripe When I got these, I thought they were for kids, but sure enough, they fit and fit perfectly! I’m 5’8” so I like to have a little added length on the hem and these look great. My only complaint and this is likely just because they’re white, but on the hem of the leg, it looks like a stripe. The pants aren’t see through on the leg (they are somewhat on the top, but that’s mostly cover by shirts), but that hem stands out. It wasn’t bad enough that I wouldn’t wear them though and almost could be considered a styling feature depending on how you look at it.

Perfect Fit I am very skinny (5'8", 114 lbs, size 0) and struggle to find skinny jeans that are tall and don't give me wedgies or feel like they are cutting off blood flow at the knee. These jeans are very comfortable. They smelled weird coming out of the packaging but the instructions advised I throw them in the wash so I have yet to wash and re-wear them. It's nice to find something casual that doesn't break the bank. I am very happy with this purchase!

Super inconsistent sizing! AWFUL quality fabric. UPDATE: ugh. just look at the new photos. Changing to 1-star. Do NOT recommend. After a few months, I’ve found that these jeans are total garbage. They are droopy and unflattering, and the material quality is extremely poor. Even freshly washed, they never un-bag. The elastic in the fabric buckles out or pulls in weird looking strips in multiple places, and it doesn’t even smooth out after putting them on. TERRIBLE! Wish I never bought them, even at my insanely cheap price. Overall: Meh. I would have returned these if they were at what appears to be the very reasonable sounding full price. - Material is very stretchy, but a bit thin. Nice for summer, but you can catch some VPL even through the dark wash. - Inseam length runs short. I wear flats and almost always have to get short lengths for a 30" inseam, but the short of these hit in an awkward spot above my ankle bone. - The listing didn't specify, but these are pretty high cut. They hit right at my belly button. - Butt pockets are higher than I expected, but not too 90s looking. I bought a pair of these in 2 short. Initial fit seemed just okay, but for the very low price I decided to grab a second identical pair in case I ended up loving them after a few wears. Turns out... They are clearance priced for a reason! In the photos, you can see two pairs of the same jean- same exact size, same exact color, but cut at least a size differently. The waists have about 1-2" difference in diameter! Luckily, the material is stretchy enough that the cut too small jeans are just good enough. And the cut is high enough that cut large ones are also just good enough. Neither are great, but for the price I found them at, I'll keep them while I wait for my normal jeans to go on sale (AE jeggings).

Size up twice! I took this awkward photo just for you guys so more people can see this! Okay I am a size 8 (30) in most Amazon brand pants! I tried a size 8- couldn’t button it. Inconsistent Sizing Between Shades Please be aware the sizing is very inconsistent between shades. I bought these in multiple colors, New Indigo Worn, Indigo Destructed, Black Stonewash and Granola (corduroy) all size 24, 100% cotton, and all with a 31” inseam. New Indigo Worn and Black Stonewash had only a 30” inseam, even though the label says 31”. The waist on these two shades was also 3/4 inch smaller than Indigo Destructed and Granola. New Indigo Worn and Black Stonewash were much shorter and had an overall much smaller fit than the other two shades. Pictures are of New Indigo Worn.

Fit small on me, should’ve ordered a size or two up. So I have to laugh because a lot of the other reviews are complaining about the jeans being too big or too short and I am having the exact opposite experience. I’m 5’3” and 175lbs. I have no butt and awkward shaped hips I’ve been told. I typically wear a size 10-12 and when I’m able to I buy “short” size. These didn’t have a short size so I followed the sizing chart which said if I wear a size 12 I should get a 31. I got a 31 and I could honestly barely button them. They would be okay as standing jeans but sitting in these would be another story for me. They are maybe an inch too long as well. I really needed probably a size 33 in the waist which is crazy because they say that’s a size 16! They also come up higher than my belly button although the model in the photo has them coming up below her belly button. I’m not sure if maybe they are hitting my waist too high because I am short or what’s happening. Maybe they need to sit lower on my waist (if they fit) but then they would be extremely too long. I love the color, I honestly like the way the jeans look on me and I felt like the bagginess wasn’t too much for me. They just didn’t fit me and either came up too high on my body shape or something. Good quality denim just a bad fit for my personal body type

Loose fitting jeans. True to size fit. My 5’5” 115 lbs teen daughter wears size 26 in Levi’s jeans and that’s the size I ordered these Levi’s baggy jeans for her. They fit her well. The waist fits comfortably and sits just below the belly button. The legs are loose fitting but not very baggy. Great jean but pants fit differently depending on color which is frustrating Buying jeans is always a trial. When I got these jeans I didn't know what to expect because previous Lee's didn't fit well. As soon as I tried these jeans on I was in love. I'm 5'9 and weight 285. I have an hourglass figure (wide hips and shoulders with a narrower waist). Most pants don't fit my thighs if they fit my hips well but these hugged everything in a flattering and comfortable way. (For this pair of pants I'm referring to the majestic style pants here). I need a pair of work pants so I decided I would order the exact same pair in black because I loved them so much. What a disappointment! They're tight on the waist and when I bend over I can feel them digging into my stomach. There's no way I would be able to sit in these for any extended period. They're literally just the tiniest bit smaller than the first pair I bought and that makes them unwearable for me. The black pants are also really long even though I got the regular length. I got the majestic in the long length (which were too long but I didn't mind) and the black pants in the normal length, but the black pants were nearly the same length! Super disappointed and returning. I would buy the majestic pair again though.

Too bad about the "saddle bags" I really like everything about Lee jeans except that no matter which version I buy, they ALWAYS seem to have extra fabric on the outside of the thighs for so-called "saddlebags." Hey! Not all women have these! I hate the little pockets this extra fabric creates--at times I feel like I'm wearing jodhpurs--and in the past I've even tried to sew them off, which has generally made them look dumb in other ways! Otherwise, these jeans are really nice. They have a comfortable degree of stretch, the length is just right (I'm 5'3", 130 lbs & ordered a 6 short), the blue color is pretty, & for the price, the quality is pretty good. I just wish Lee would ditch the extra fabric on the sides--not all women's figures are the same!

My favorite jeans! I'm 5'4", 150 lbs, 34" waist, with all my squishy weight in my stomach. I have very slim hips and legs, and wear a short in pants. I always struggle to find skinny jeans that fit my legs AND my waist. These are absolutely perfect in almost every way. The sizing is a little off. I normally wear a size 8 but a size 6 fits way better. They are very stretchy but hold their shape incredibly well. The legs fit like skinny jeans are supposed to. My only complaints are that they only have two colors available in my size (6 short), and the second pair of the Sofia color that I got fit slightly different than the first pair... the legs aren't quite as fitted, and the color is darker. Overall these are my absolute favorite jeans, so I still gave them a 5 star rating. I'm waiting and hoping that they eventually start stocking the other colors in this size.

Love the Fit I just received my Angel Forever Young jeans today and so far I am very please with them. I ordered the Sophia (indigo blue) color. The second picture is to dark, they are the color of the first picture. They have a nice embroidery on the back pockets and are distressed. I ordered a 16W and they fit perfect. I have the same brand in capris and they have worn and washed well. I am 5’4” and 195lbs. I usually take a 16-18 regular but, I wanted to try a 16W because in the stores, the jeans seem to be running small and I’m glad I did. I would definitely say these run true to size. They have the waistband and tummy control I like and they are a high rise. Also they are true skinny jeans. Now, I have not washed or worn them yet and I have read some of the reviews that concern me, i.e. first wash and had developed holes or worn a few times and developed holes and the seams just split. I have looked at all the seams and waistband and didn’t see any problems. I will update this review if there are any issues with mine, I sure hope not. My experience in the past has been fabulous with this brand.

Great material, weird fit I'm 5'6" and 135-140 with a thick waist and narrow hips. I'd normally go for a 6 but based on the waist measurements in the size chart, I sized up to an 8. I shouldn't have! First things first, I really wish I had gotten a 6. It's super stretchy fabric and the 8 is wayyyy too big in the butt/hips (not super surprising but disappointing) and somehow also too big in the waist! (I swear my waist is at least a 29 so the 8 should NOT be big on me.) In addition, if I hike up the waist to my actual natural waist (so the crotch isn't weird and droopy) they are sooooo short on me. I might have very slightly long legs for my height but not crazy long by any means, and I almost never find that pants are too short on me. I know these aren't supposed to be the 7 for All Mankind early '00s Dojo style that literally drags on the ground, but these don't even hit the top of my foot. In the photo, I don't have them hiked up all the way. Overall, I'm bummed because the material is really nice and the style is actually really cute—it's just too baggy up top to be flattering and too short to let me feel comfortable wearing them. Hope it works better for others!

Don't hesitate to get these! I really wish these jeans came in petite sizes. I'm 5 foot 3 and 110 pounds. I knew they would be large on me, but they are so stinkin' cute! The size 4 will work, but I'll need to find the right belt to hold them in place without bunching up the waistline. I wanted a slightly baggy fit anyway. They are also a little longer than I'd prefer, but they don't drag on the ground when I wear shoes. I have always been a fan of the button fly, and this one looks really nice. It's high waisted, so it could be long in the crotch if you decide to size up like I did. There is plenty of pocket space in the front and back. The rear end on my pair has a slightly faded look, but it's not dramatic. I know I've said some critical things about these jeans, but all of the problems I have can be attributed to the fact that a smaller size would fit me better. The jeans themselves look fantastic, very fashionable! If they can look this good on someone who doesn't exactly fit them, imagine how good they will look on a person who does!

Stretchy, soft and comfortable. These wide leg, high waisted jeans are very comfortable. They fit me well, except because of my shortness (5' 4") they are not really cropped on me. More like flood pants, but I am okay with that. I do not have to worry about the bottoms getting wet or my lower legs getting too cold, lol. They have three buttons that are quite stiff to unbutton when you get them but are fine after the first time. The zipper works well. There are four functional pockets, two in front and two in back. The pockets are a little smallish, but would hold a tissue or car keys. As you can see in one of my pictures, if you are apple shaped like I am, the white of the inside of the pocket (on the front) will show, despite the fit being not too tight. Not a problem for me as I most always like to wear shirts / blouses that are long enough to cover at least most of my bottom. Not sure how the pocket issue could have been prevented in the designing of them, but there probably is a way. These jeans seem to be mostly well made. The only thing I noticed really was that the buttons are sort of the cheap type that wiggle and rattle. Not sure what the durability would be of those. Aside from that, I can recommend these jeans and I am happy with them. The reason I only gave four stars was for the buttons and the white of the pocket showing. If anything comes up about those buttons on mine, I will update my review.

Ridiculously wide legs, inseam shorter than given on size chart I ordered these in the “Blue” color in size Medium. I typically wear a size 8 in pants, but the size chart could have had me in a Large instead. However, the listing says the model is “wearing size Medium, Hips 40 / Height 5’3” / Weight 136. I’m about an inch and half taller, weigh about the same, and hips are maybe an inch smaller. The Medium fits me very well and is comfortable. The inseam is at least an inch shorter than the stated 26.38” (I measured it at about 25”). The fabric has a nice weight and is soft and stretchy. The waistband is beautiful and also comfortable. It seems like it would retain its size and shape, however, I have not worn them long enough to test this theory. There are no belt loops to hold them up should they stretch out, or if they would be too loose on you. The buttons seem a little loose, but don’t show any indication of coming off.Now, I know these are advertised as wide leg, but they have W-I-D-E legs! Since they are denim which has structure, If I stand with my feet about hip width apart (a natural stance), the legs of these jeans cross over each other, which looks rather ridiculous. The seams go crooked and it looks like I'm not standing straight even though I am. I have to stand with my legs spread strangely apart for them to look as they do on the model. If only I could slim down the legs, these would be attractive, but as they are, I just can’t see myself ever wearing them, not even just around the house, as the legs crash together with every step. I’ve included photos of how they look on me (5’4.5”, 30 waist, 39 hip, about 29 inseam), and also some with a measuring tape to show the actual measurements of the size Medium. I hope this helps in your decision.

Cute jeans :) Hey there, so I don't usually buy clothes online because they simply don't fit; but I have learned to buy larger then I typically would need and it works out. In this case I ordered XL and they are a very comfortable fit. I am 5 ft 6 in and about 170 lb; I'm a little "hippy" at 41" and that's where jeans usually don't work for me. But these are a little loose, which I like, and stretchy; so I have mobility, which is another important factor. I did immediately notice that the open stars on the jeans look like little stickers you would just peel and stick in your notebook, but I have washed and dried them and they stayed on. I would imagine they won't last forever, but they would look fine without them, or I could put something else in their place. The material underneath the holes is like a microfiber, so it's thin, breathable and stretchy. I will be ordering some of the other designs for sure.

Amazing and Comfortable! Not Stylish... on me...BUT I love them. I bought these for work. I love them for work! I needed 100% cotton jeans and these did the trick. They’re not super flattering but boy are they comfortable! So great for lounging, running errands, they’d have FANTASTIC for gardening, and they are great contract/construction work. I wore them when I visited power plants for work and I will wear them again for gardening and other outdoor adventures when it’s hot out and I need long pants. I love these pants!! I wish they were a little more stylish so I could wear them more often... I mean... I could... but I don’t have that lien or courage... yet. BUT, I will wear these every opportunity I get and maybe I can find a way to make them stylish. I think it’s my body. Some women could definitely make these stylish. I’m a little shorter and more petite. I think for these to be stylish you probably need longer legs. I have to to roll these at the bottom and I look like a little girl. I also got the smallest size they offered and they get a little roomy... they may be made to be that way though. Anyway, I seriously do love these. Comfiest and most breathable jeans that I have ever worn. I want MORE 100% cotton jeans. Please!! They truly are amazing. GET THEM. They may look great on you, just not as much on me. If the way don’t look “cute,” keep them for fun and rugged activities/gardening. P.S. I got a size 4. This was the lowest offered when I bought them. They stay up because of where they sit on my hips though. I’m something like a 27in waist, 34-35inch hips?? Waist size doesn’t matter as much for these... or they didn’t for me... I’m 5’3”

I’m a little shorter and more petite. I think for these to be stylish you probably need longer legs. I have to to roll these at the bottom and I look like a little girl. I also got the smallest size they offered and they get a little roomy... they may be made to be that way though. Anyway, I seriously do love these. Comfiest and most breathable jeans that I have ever worn. I want MORE 100% cotton jeans. Please!! They truly are amazing. GET THEM. They may look great on you, just not as much on me. If the way don’t look “cute,” keep them for fun and rugged activities/gardening. P.S. I got a size 4. This was the lowest offered when I bought them. They stay up because of where they sit on my hips though. I’m something like a 27in waist, 34-35inch hips?? Waist size doesn’t matter as much for these... or they didn’t for me... I’m 5’3”

Run a bit big I love levis! I have some shorter 501’s that I’ve had for a few years but I am at the age where longer shorts are definitely more appropriate for most events. I tried a size 25 based off the size chart and found them way too big, so I exchanged for a 24 and they are perfect. I line dry most of my clothes so shrinkage is unlikely, but if they do happen to shrink, I still have some space all around to account for that without being too baggy. *If you dry these, they will shrink.* 501’s are not pre shrunk because they are designed to be “broke in” by sitting in a tub of water with the jeans on, and wearing them until they dry. They are not meant to be washed every wear or even every two wears, so keep that in mind! I’m gonna put my measurements at the bottom of this so hopefully it will help some pick the correct size. Anyways, get these, you’ll love them! I find them very flattering on everyone I see wearing them:) • My measurements in inches: Waist-25 Hip-31.5 Widest point of hips(butt)-35 Thigh at widest-20.5

I was looking for fleece-lined leggings for when I move to the mountains this winter, and I’ll be spending a ton of time outdoors through my work, but also working in a very casual-dress office environment as well. I’ve lived in the area before, and I needed to stock up on pieces I can layer. I seriously just ordered three more pairs of these leggings, because they’re on sale, and the cabin I’ll be living in has access to a washer/dryer, but our buildings operate on solar power, and to conserve our energy I’ll likely drive to the laundromat every few weeks, and these are the most comfortable, attractive, versatile leggings I own. I can layer them with my other thermal tights/leggings for extra warmth if needed in the office, or layer them under ski-pants or thicker hiking pants outside. Also, I just wore them to lunch with a friend today, they look fantastic, so I just wore them with a long-sleeved more dressy top and they were great on their own. I am 5’4” and 128-130 lbs, and curvy hourglass shape, more curvy on the top though. My regular bottom half measurements are included in the photos I’ve attached. The mediums fit very well, even though most of my leggings are smalls, I wanted something looser and able to layer over another pair of fleece leggings for cold days - and these leggings are soft and so stretchy that I can comfortably wear them alone or layer below with another thin thermal layer. I love that they’re lined leggings, but especially since I sized up a little, they aren’t inappropriately clingy. The waistband is soft, stretchy, and not constricting at all. The waistband on the medium is a little loose on me, but not in a way that they’re baggy or would fall down, or that anyone would notice. Im curled up on the couch writing this right now, and regardless of how I sit or lay down, they’re comfortable and flexible. The leggings are opaque and not sheer at all. They’ll provide a great “blue jean” looking cover that I can wear my super stretchy fleece lined leggings under if needed. I just bought another pair of the blue, and two pairs of the black. Thank you to the poster that mentioned piling. Because of it, I washed these the same way I wash my more traditional looking fleece lined leggings - in mesh laundry bags on a cold delicate cycle and then hang to dry. I’ll update my review if there’s any issue with piling with this laundry routine, but I haven’t had any yet.

Pleasantly Surprised! I read a lot of the reviews so that I’d have a better understanding on what size pants I should order. I bought them in a size large and they fit me so nicely. I don’t typically wear jeans, I never liked having to button my pants up so I usually just wear leggings. I primarily dress for comfort. I absolutely loved the way they looked on me. My favorite part about these pants is that you don’t have to button or zip them up because the waist is stretchy and you can tie them however tight you’d want the waist area to be. I don’t feel restricted in these pants, they’re not too long and they’re not too short either. I’m 5’6 and weigh approximately 170lbs so I’m very glad I went with a size large. I definitely recommend getting these jeans and order them in your actual size. I don’t see a reason why you’d have to size up with these jeans. I want to get them in other colors! EDIT: To add an update: So I’ve bought these pants in most of the colors they offer and I still love them. They are easily on my list of favorite jeans to wear. They are just so comfortable, I can squat down and the jeans move with me because they’re so stretchy! Absolutely fantastic jeans :)

Very comfy... Ok... first, I gotta say: these pants are super cute and comfy. I have always had a harder time shopping for pants, because of my extremely curvy figure. Usually, if it fits my hips, it's too big for my waist. These hug me in all the right places. I am 5'7" 270lbs with 55" hips! My shape is in between hourglass and pear. Typically, in stretch pants, I wear a size 2x to 3x depending on the brand. In jeans I wear a size 24. So, I purchased these in 4x. They are just right (provided they don't shrink in the dryer!). I cannot comment on durability, as I only got them today and only tried them on to see if they fit and take pics for your review. If anything changes I'll update. However, right now I do recommend these and now I think I want to get a couple other colors, as well. Update: they did not shrink in the dryer on standard low heat. The only thing that I don't like is the elastic bad twists, however they still seem to lay flat once I get them pulled up. So far I still like them and will probably order other colors in the near future.

Decent Jeans For reference: I am 5'8", 190ish lbs, pear shape with a meno-belly, 31" inseam, 37" waist, 45" hips. I typically wear 14/16 depending on the style and brand. I did look at their size chart and although my measurements did not match up I decided to try size 14 Tall. After washing on cold and drying on low heat I can say they fit. The inseam on the pair I received is 30", which works fine for me. These are snug around the waist but loosen up as you wear them. A 16 would have been too big/baggy, so with this jean I believe I am between the two sizes. There is a good weight to these but not so heavy they cannot be worn. Will be good Fall and Winter jeans, but too heavy for Summer. These are high waisted and indeed wide legged as described. These may be tummy controlled for smaller tummies, but without additional help from shapewear undergarments these do not help the meno-belly. Not a complaint, just an fyi. Overall I find these to be decent quality jeans for a reasonable price.

Soft and comfy These pants are incredibly comfortable. The waist covers my belly button, gives a nice slimming effect, and is also stretchy enough to not dig into my belly when I sit down. I ordered a pair in brown, and the color is a nice chocolate shade. The length is nice also; at 5’10”, I was worried that the 31” inseam would be too short, but they fall well past my ankles. These are high quality and look nice. Very happy!

Not a style for me but very nice quality The denim is really nice weight in these high waist jeans. No visible panty lines, they’re not so heavy that they are hot to wear and have just enough stretch for them to keep their shape. The seams and hems are uniformly stitched and sturdy also. The legs are very wide, as shown, but I don’t feel that they have a baggy fit elsewhere. Unfortunately, on my body shape and height I don’t find them to be at all flattering I don’t know if it is the back pocket placement, the leg length or the snug fit of the jeans from the waist to the hips. (I’m 5’4” tall and wearing a size 10.) Even with a 2”heel I felt I looked short and squat. Not a look I can successfully carry off. On a more slender, taller frame they would be very cute.

Definitely size up! Pay CLOSE attention to the size chart! I think the size chart might be pretty accurate. I am 5'4" and about 200 pounds. I usually wear around a size 14 but I got the 16 just in case because of the measurements. These are still too small and much tighter than I like. The only reason they even still fit is because of how stretchy they are. So don't expect the sizes to match what you are used to. Check the size chart. If these were the right size for me I think I would like them ok. The stretchy material should be very comfortable. The length is good for me. The zippers is not smooth and takes some effort. It gets caught up a lot. The pockets are not very deep and the fabric feels like it won't last very long. The size on the pants says 32 everywhere. Nowhere on the pants does it have the size 16. Only on the plastic wrapping does it say size 16. So it isn't surprising that this doesn't match closer to actual US sizes. Overall it is an ok pair of pants. Just ok though.

It is entitled a “leather belt” but down further in the description it states “faux leather” so truly not leather but I wasn’t expecting it to be for $15.00. Still it’s false advertising, hence my 4 stars. Besides that the rest is all positive for the belt I paid for. I’m a 37” waist & went with a large & it wasn’t too large for me. There are 6 holes to the belt & I use hole #5 to buckle it. The material is nice & smooth. There isn’t a flaw to it. It’s 1” wide & the buckle is 1-1/2” wide. The buckle is a nice silver which is shiny but not too shiny. It does come with instructions-per my pictures- as well as a little gadget to use in case the belt is too long. The gadget allows you to unscrew the buckle & cut the belt to shorten it. I’d say the sizing they provide is accurate though. I could’ve gone with a medium. Overall a great belt to wear with jeans or dress pants. A nice color black. I’m happy with this belt, it fits comfortably & I feel it was well worth what I paid. I definitely recommend it.

Great Fit I love the wide leg and the length is great. (I’m 5’4” and they don’t drag as much as most.) I like the stretch and the color. The material is thick and the seams are holding very well. They are comfortable but I do need to get used to have no pockets. I think they’re a good value.

Nice length crop, light summer weight denim These are a nice light pair of summer weight denim pants. I think they're meant to be crops, but I am 5'3"and these came down to my ankles (which I prefer anyway). The material is generally quite soft but some of the seams are very rough, especially around the waist area. It's like they chose to burn the thread ends rather than tie them off. I would make sure to always tuck in my shirt with these or wear substantial undergarments that come up to the waist (at least) to protect your skin. The thread roughness aside, I like the stretch of these pants. I wish they were a bit more structured in the midsection. Since they are so very high waisted, one would hope they would provide some tummy control, but these just move and stretch with you, which makes them incredibly comfortable. The green is very light and makes for easy pairing with many colors of tops and shoes. I normally wear an 8 or 29 and these pants in a large fit me well.

Very stylish and stretchy (but stitching coming undone after 2 1/2 months) But they're not so stretchy that they become misshapen or stretch out too much. The high waist and tight front panel help snatch you in. I wash them in cold water and air-dry them, so no issues with bleeding or shrinking. I'm 5'8" so the hem hits right above my ankle. These pants look fantastic with ankle boots. Love the dark blue color. Edit: Went from 5 stars to 3 as the stitching on the right back pocket is coming undone. It's strange because I haven't use the right pocket with more frequency than the left. It's a bummer because these jeans are cute. I wish they were constructed better,

New favorite pants! These are the best pants! I love the fabric, the fit, and I'll be ordering more asap. I got the size M in the color white and they fit me well. I am typically size 4-6, 29" waist, 41" hips, 25" inseam, 5'0" tall. These fit me perfectly and have plenty of stretch so I could have possibly sized down to the S. The white jeans are not transparent or see through. They wash and dry well. I cannot say enough good things about these pants, especially considering how hard jeans are to find when you are petite. Can't wait to get the other colors! Worth every penny!

GREAT JEANS But-Be Sure to ORDER a MUCH LARGER SIZE than you usually wear! Go Up one or 2 sizes! I LOVE, LOVE, LOVE having Real Pockets in Front And Back! Love the Light Blue Color and The GENEROUS 4-Way STRETCH Fabric. These Fit me Tight but I can still squeeze into them and they are very Flattering. Not Uncomfortable but they make me look Skinner than I am and comfortably hold my Tummy In Flat! I am 5'4" HEIGHT + WEIGHT 150 lbs. / I normally order a size Large (14-16) in any other brand of Jeggings / Pants/Jeans. *(HOWEVER, I did read all the Previous Comments before me and listened because most of them advised to order UP A Larger Size)...So, I ordered the Large 2X size and I can wear them but they are a bit tight and if I lost 5 lbs they would fit 100% perfect. I have now ordered a Larger Size of 3X and it will arrive soon--Shipping was VERY Fast on my First Pair. Only took a few days to arrive. I will absolutely keep the original pair, not sending back cuz I can still fit in them (tight), and if I lost 5 lbs..well, this is an incentive for me. The Jeans are really nice and look great and feel great, albeit a little tight at the moment. *The Only Thing I didnt Like was the material is a type of "distressed fabric" look in a few areas were ligther than others, it was that way on BOTH the pants I ordered, so it wasnt a flaw-some people might like that "distressed, used, worn out fabric look" but I dont. However, Everything Else on them is so Perfect and Oh so Soft, that I had to overlook it.

Soft and Versatile I bought a medium, based on reviews. I weigh about 120 and am about 5' 5" - or a little under. The jeggings are a little loose, but a small would have been too small. If need be, I will sew in a tuck or two. Generally speaking - these fit as expected. The rise is 10". I will trim the bottoms to fit my exact height, and won't bother hemming them. They have enough polyester in them I don't think there will unravel. / My pair: the waist-band twists in the wash, so I had to sew a few stitches to get it stay in place. Definitely a down-side.

Comfortable I forgot that I was wearing denim. This felt more like tights or yoga pants for me. With it being black, you can't really tell that its denim either. It's stretchy material and hugs your body just right. The length is just right for 5'6" height.

Sparkly Belt This belt is super cute. It's very sparkly and stylish. The rhinestones on the buckle are a great touch! The belt is the perfect width to wear with jeans. It is thick and of great quality. My only complaint is with the sizing. I am a waist 28 so I ordered a size small which is supposed to fit waist 26-34. The medium is 32-40. This belt barely fits; I can only get it on the first hole and it feels tight. There is no way that this would fit someone with a 34 waist. The size chart is way off. I wish I had ordered a medium, but I didn't know that the chart was incorrect. I would recommend sizing up from whatever size you are on the size chart. I wish I had known that before ordering it. Overall, I do like this belt, but I will have limited use of it because of the tight fit.

Gorgeous band, but buckle's not subtle This silver "sequin" belt has a gorgeous band. It's a nice thick quality leather covered in multi-shades of silver glitter (that doesn't shed), outlined in silver beading ( not sequins). I adore the belt band, just wish the buckle wasn't 3 " wide and covered in VERY large rhinestones that seem a lil cheesy for an older woman like me. I just wear my shirt untucked in the front to camoflage the buckle, and got LOTS of compliments from girlfriends wearing it as slightly more demure bling. I have a 30" waist and size 26-34 fits me perfectly (with 2 belt-holes to spare if I gainore flab), and the belt "tail" lies nice and flat against me. I do luv this fun belt!

Color/Style A+, Not Longlasting/Durable, Thin, Long 140 5'5' - I ordered a S, M, and L. Large was too big, I returned it. Style is nice. Color is as shown. Quality is ok, material is thin. Durability is less than a year. Medium fit a bit loose but comfortable, good fit after washed, looser with wear. The waist is still too stretchy, so I have to wear a belt all the time or it will fall down and my belly will stick out. It is long on me, about 2.5 inches below my ankle, so I have to fold it over or wear platform shoes for the material not to drag on the floor. I really like the flare and I have meaty thighs, so the stretch is good. I wear the medium most, i have had it for 8 months and worn it liberally, the denim on the inner thighs is already wearing down like shown in the pic, and will have a holes very soon and won't be wearable anymore. So the quality and durability of these is only about 8-10 months if you wear them often and your thighs touch. So that is diappointing. The small I also kept. Small was still large at the waist that it will fall down without a belt & can create a muffin top of you are curvy in the belly. The small fit snug, material stretches, so fits my thighs, but is a bit snug which is not my favorite look. Still long, so I fold it up when wearing flats and leave it long when wearing platforms.

Highly recommend.

Size down but shockingly good!! I am really impressed with these jeans! I am truly shocked at how much I like them! I did cut the bottom as they were slightly longer than I like, but now they are perfect! I already purchased the other two colors! Some thoughts below: - Runs large. I usually wear a size 6 or 28 in jeans and I went with a 4 in these and they are PERFECT! I am 5'7" and 135lbs for reference. - The length is a bit longer than I like so I cut about 1.5" off the bottom. The photo in heels is full length and the other two photos are cut. I might cut a bit more off but have not decided yet. Even with the shorter length, they still look cute with heels or flats! - The material is somewhat soft but still holds its shape. It has a bit of stretch to it, but not enough that its going to stretch out after one wear. - Nice and high waisted which is what I think makes the wider leg work. - These are tight through the thigh but wide through the calf, they're perfect! Overall, I would size down one size, especially if you are between sizes. Expect the length to be around 29" inseam but cutting them is super easy if you want them shorter and they still look cute! Definitely recommend.

These are perfect for petite ppl with some curves! I’m only 5’1 petite and 126 pounds. I have a little bit bigger of a bottom though and it’s hard to find jeans that are not too long but also fit my waist and curve’s comfortably. I have bought $200 jeans and many other high end ones that never really meet my expectations. So I wasn’t too hopeful on these either but to my surprise they’re my favorite jeans now! The material is so soft, they have the perfect amount of stretch in them they hug my curves nicely, the length is perfect, and the button is sewn on super strong! The color is exactly as it appears online and the size chart was super accurate. The threading is nice too. I did wash them on normal cycle, cool water, and dried the n medium heat, and I had no issues with shrinking, fading, or loose threads after. Cute but poor quality control In general these are cute & fit me well. I ordered “E Deep Blue” in both Med & Large. I’m 5’2” & 164lbs, typically size 8-10 & the Med fit me best. I didn’t notice much difference in the Large. The hip area was a bit more roomy, and I noticed the extra fabric in the crotch area in the Large, but that wasn’t an issue with the Medium. As you can see from the pics, the colors are very inconsistent! I ordered the SAME color/style in 2 sizes but they looked quite different. (Large is darker on left.) Second pic is of labels. I think they are the correct items since the only difference is the last 2 digits under the bar code, which I’m assuming may represent size? I still recommend giving these a try, but order more than one like I did & return what doesn’t work.

Love these overalls but… (Before I start, I’m 5ft 4, 138lbs, Size Medium, Size 9, 29 waist of this helps at all?) The first 2 photos is what the problem is for most people — the extra material at the crotch region. I somewhat don’t mind this because if I’m on the time of the month, these are great for the wiggle room if I’m bloated. But it can look unflattering! The 3rd photo is length. They’re perfect, but I roll may cuffs by habit anyways, so, that’s where the 4th photo kicks in. I like the cuff look better on me. The last photo is another angle focusing on the material in that region. I am planning to buy another in a small (same color) and perhaps that will be better so it won’t have the stretchy pouch there. I’ve read some reviews where alot of women are saying these aren’t stretchy? Perhaps it differs for color/style? Because these are STRETCHY. Best way to describe these overalls: The material for Jeggings. They look like Jean material but stretchy asf. Are these worth $40+? No. I wish they was alittle cheaper. Luckily for me, a flash sale popped up and I got them less than $40.

I love these overalls! These overalls are medium weight denim. They have a bit of a stretch. I have them rolled up twice, so in photo they are about 2-3 inches shorter than they would be if left unrolled. I like it that they are tapered at the ankle area. The older overalls from the 1970’s had a much wider leg. I think the bit of tapering makes them more flattering. I used to live in overalls when I was young and wanted to find a pair that I could wear now in my 60’s. I was surprised to get a compliment on them from a 20something year old salesperson at the mall. Yesterday I got another compliment from a 50something yr old woman so I thought I should leave a review. I haven’t gotten any compliments on anything I’ve worn in ages! I love the comfortable look & feel. I will be buying a backup pair in case Amazon does away with this one. They meet all of my criteria for great overalls! My height is 5’3” weight is 155#.

LOVE THESE. Very impressed! Honestly, I did not not hold out much hope for these. The shipping took a long time (although hey were clear about that up front) they still arrived early based on the window they gave me. They are not the color showed in the ad, they are a little darker...BUT OMG I LOVEEEE THEM! They are super soft, plenty long, good material. They are a little baggy (which is why I got the large - I am 5'8" and about 160lb) and they honestly fit like a dream. Overalls usually don't look right on me, I am kind of pear shape, large bottoms and small tops, but these are amazing. Worth the wait. My boyfriend loves them and asked me why I have not been wearing overalls my whole life. I got them to wear when we go to his lease to hunt and work but I swear I am going to wear these everywhere. Keep in mind, there are a few 'styles' on there. Mine are the more basic ones - not the cutesie stylish ones on there. But they are incredible. I did not want to take them off to wash them.

Missing button, extra fabric, bad overall (pun intended) I got the XL in one of the black colors. I'm 5'4" and about 180 lbs. Approximately 36" waist, 43" hips, 40" bust. I'm gonna list the cons first, then the pros, because the cons outweigh the pros. Cons: - Missing button on the right side, loose button was nowhere to be found - Tons of extra fabric on crotch/lower stomach area, making it look weird when I bend the slightest bit - These were most likely returned and resold, as they came with the legs already rolled up (surely from a previous customer) and they had a strong scent of laundry detergent. They were probably returned due to the missing button. Who can blame 'em. - Packaged in just a clear plastic bag, making the contents more susceptible to stealing and damage - Fabric is pretty thin Pros: - Good stretch - Cute, if you squint hard enough - Mostly good fit - Good price, but... y'know. I would not recommend these. Sketchy. Go figure. Maybe I'll bring them to a tailor which will end up costing me in total the same amount I would've paid if I'd just spent a bit more on a legit brand. I'd return them but who can be bothered. Hopefully if I do keep them they'll hold up, but I don't trust it. Yayyy

As comfy as leggings, but thicker & dressier (and no cameltoe) As w/ seemingly many others, I bought my first pairs @ CVS (got 3). I don’t normally shop for clothes there, but they were a serendipitous discovery, and having a hard-to-fit body where I don’t often find items that I can wear off-the-rack (I’m an endomorph, or “apple” type, where my waist is short & thick), I wanted to look for more pairs, solids specifically (black, heather grey & indigo w/ gold threading), to have as wardrobe basics. I was THRILLED to find them on Amazon (and they do have their own website, as well), and bought the pairs that I was looking for, after reading reviews from customers who ended up becoming Lildy fans in the same manner. These jeggings are SO comfy that you can literally wear them 24/7, and even for activities that require a broad range of movement (splits, high kicks). I wear them to sleep, because I’m at that stage in my life where I’m too lazy to change in & out of clothes constantly (just turned 47). I have a “uniform” that works for me (wifebeater & jeggings), and it’s nice to be able to simply add accessories, outerwear & shoes, and voila….I’m dressed to go outside. 👌 Prior to finding the Lildy jeggings @ CVS, I was wearing leggings, but would wear a mini over them solely for the coverage to hide the cameltoe. Now, I don’t have to do that because the material for the jeggings is thicker. I save myself from needing to put on an extra garment that constantly shifts from movement, and requires additional labor to adjust & launder. Dressing is much easier, and I effortlessly look pulled together. I always wore bootcut or flared pants (they were more flattering for my silhouette), but these jeggings fit so well that I feel confident about how I look in them (can’t say the same for the ones from Walmart), and unlike w/ flared or bootcut bottoms, these tuck into knee-hi boots w/o any of that extra fabric, and make looking cute such a non-job. Highly recommended, if you feel that the cut will work for your body shape. Great value for the price—you’ll get hooked & end up coming back for more colors & patterns. (For reference, I’m just under 5’2”, ~110 lbs., 28” waist & 28” inseam.) UPDATE: (May 2022) I’m now on my SEVENTH pair of Lildy jeggings! 👖 Just got the olive green solid color. Made just like the rest. I LIVE in these—you can exercise & sleep in these jeggings, and it’s nice that they look like “real pants.” 😆 The grey ones look fairly refined, almost like twill pants, and you just pull’em up like they’re you’re favorite pair of leggings, but might be fine for a “casual nice” work dress code. 🥰

Perfect Fit - Very Cute I’m 5’4” and 128 lbs with a very curvy, hour glass shape, and these fit and look great. I probably could have gone up a size, they’re a little snug, but I still was able to layer a pair of fleece leggings underneath and tuck in a thermal top - which is exactly what I needed jeans for. I’ll be taking an extended camping/hiking trip out west, and I wanted some casual jeans that I could layer over thermal underwear when I’m around our (unheated but indoor) base camp, or while going into town. These are a perfect length on me, and while straight-skinny legged jeans don’t always look great on my figure - I feel like I look great in these, and that’s what matters.

Red lined front pockets NOT flattering and jeans have unusual fabric I’m 5’5 129 pounds and I ordered the medium despite being a small but that was the size that was recommended. These have a lot of stretch to them. I washed in washer and dryer and there was definitely some shrinkage. When putting on after washing they fit okay except the waist was a little big. The jeans have two pockets in the front and I should have known better than to order pants with pockets in the front where the lower stomach is. Most women, especially of a particular age 😜, do not need anything like pockets to accentuate a part of our body that we don’t actually “want” to be accentuated. Not only that, there are red lines on the pockets drawing the eye further to that area that does not need to be accentuated 😜. I don’t usually order clothes off of Amazon but I have had some luck recently with a few orders of pants I really liked. Furthermore, the pants have the strangest texture. It’s very difficult to describe. I know that I don’t care for the texture and haven’t run into anything like it in any other clothing I have owned. I also think after one wash, they looked like they were about 10 years old already. In short, these jeans fell way short in comfort (weird texture), style, and quality.

Cute jeans, so-so quality The seams on these jeans give them a cute, modern touch and stylistically go great with the bell bottoms. They're really stretchy, which is also a bonus. I ordered them in "darkness blue." I'm 5'4", 157 pounds and the large fits comfortably. Most of my height is in my torso (read: I have short legs) so I had to wear really high shoes for these not to drag the ground. I don't consider that a drawback, though. Bell bottoms should never be worn as floods, in my opinion. Finally, it's incredibly hard to button and unbutton these jeans -- they just aren't terribly well made. For the price point, maybe that's to be expected, but I took off a star for it anyway.

Nice fitting, middle-weight jeans. Perfect length for someone 5'4"! These are a well made pair of jeans that surprised me with a little more flare than I had anticipated. I thought it was a small flare, but when I got them I found they are full fledged bell-bottoms. That is fine with me. I loved bell-bottoms when I was growing up (yes, I am that old, lol) but my mom mostly did not allow me to have them, so I was thrilled the past 15 years or so when they have been in style a few times. They are of medium weight and softness. The stitching is solid and looks nice on them. I like the seam that runs down the front of them. I can remember a long while back when that was a style too. They have 2 front and 2 back pockets, all of them functional. I got the X-Large and they fit me very nicely with a bit of room to spare due to the 2% elastane in the material. The color is really nice and I think it is just right for what I was looking for in a pair of jeans. Another nice thing is that the length for my 5'4" height is perfect without needing to be shortened. I am very pleased with these jeans and happy that I got them! Definitely can recommend these for nice looking and comfortable jeans.

Great fit, why was there a rock in the pocket? I absolutely love the fit of these jeans because they have so much stretch, they're so comfortable, it doesn't even feel like you're wearing jeans. I live in pajamas most of the time, so I just can't tolerate uncomfortable jeans anymore, and these are wonderful. One weird thing, though- I was super perplexed to put my hands in the front pockets while trying them on and finding a grape-sized rock in the left front pocket. I attached a photo because it was so bizarre. There were no tags on the pants when I received them, so now I'm wondering if I got a pair someone else returned (after wearing them on a rock-collecting excursion, I suppose) because I can't imagine how else a rock gets into the pocket of a new pair of jeans. I'd also note that these are going to be very long on a petite person. I'm 5'4" and I'll only be able to wear these with heels or heeled boots because otherwise I trip over the hems.

Bootcut Where? These are literally the most perfect 70’s style bell bottom/flare jeans on my 5’3” frame! Flattering high rise creates the illusion of a smaller waist, even if you don’t have one. Nice medium weight denim, perfect blue shade to match everything, and very, very stretchy. So stretchy in fact, that you could easily go down one size I think. So many compliments 😍. Love these!!!

Bigger than bootcut, BIG legs on these jeans. I'm 5'4", 147 lbs, 29/39 and chose a size 12 in these jeans. Overall the fit is great, these are very comfortable to wear, and I like the Bluing color of these jeans, but I feel that the leg type is misrepresented. The legs of these jeans are more of a stovepipe design, not nearly as fitted and shaped as the images suggest, and I don't like how huge and stumpy they make my already thick legs look. I didn't expect a close fitting pair of jeans, but neither was I expecting something so loose and baggy on me. These remind me somewhat of a certain brand of oversized leg jeans from the 90s, and I only wore those when I was a size 4-5 to avoid looking like a wall of denim. These make me look stumpy and stubby, and as much as I like how they move and feel I just don't like the look of them on. The legs of these jeans look short in the images, but mine do line up as described in the size chart, just shy of 30". These are about an inch too long for me, but I'm used to that. The material is quite stretchy, and very comfortable to move around in. I can do squats, take long strides, and stretch my legs out as much as I can in these jeans without discomfort. The waist is very comfortable and flattens my problem abdomen without digging in, and these jeans look great under close fitting tops. The backseam doesn't ride up my generously sized posterior, and the seat of these is somewhat flattering on my large backside. The front pockets are wide and deep enough for an earbuds case, but won't fit any of my phones. There are no side pockets, just front and back ones. I did have an issue with the stitching, however. There are many areas where the machine spat out knots of thread into rats nests, there's spots where the stitching was attempted to be backstitched but veered off, gaps in the stitch lines, and loose sections of threads that hang in a gap while being stitched down on either side. The four attached images are a sampling of the problems that I found in the stitching. I am not fully satisfied with the construction quality of these jeans. Overall I like the jeans enough to wear them camping or to work outdoors in cooler weather, but I'm not happy with appearance of them on and how rough the stitching is on these pants. Sidefeel Women's Wide Leg Jeans High Waist Flare Bootcut Jeans Bell Bottom Pants

Trendy jeans for all the seasons Unheard of, jeans that fit the first try! Most of my jeans are size 6, I figured M 8/10 would be a safe bet knowing these jeans are 71% cotton, another plus. I had my doubts when I saw the tube shaped package they arrived in. I tried them on pre and post laundering. Washed in cold and hung to dry, as per directions. I’m too nervous to put them in the dryer + the tag states not to. No shrinkage to . I’m 5’7”, 138 and long waisted, so these do not fit very high rise on me, which I’m fine with. The denim has a nice give and stretch - thankfully. I am not a fan of jeans that aren’t a little stretchy. Had a hard time choosing the color, this is ‘darkness blue’, it offers some leeway if they fade over time. Plus, I feel the darker colors can be more flattering as well. I think these are a nicely styled, quality pant and are on trend. Easy to wear year round with a variety of tops and shoes. Four usable pockets and belt loops. Very pleased with all aspects of these new jeans!

The front pockets are plenty deep to securely carry my smart phone. There are also 2 standard pockets on the back. Vintage looking, soft, and roomy.

BOOTY with FLARE and makes your legs look SLIM AS HELL!!! So I found this particular jean/pant in Macy's and bought during one of their "sales". Nedless to say, the pull on feature with no zipper in front but pockets in the back are great. Definitely can dress up or down depending on what you got going on. Anyway, thought I'd look on Prime Day for these since they had a limited number in my size not realizing they also came in black jean. When I tell you these pants are THE BOMB DOT COM I am not lying! They kicked my booty up, streched in all the right places, and for someone who is 5ft 2in/5ft 3in, the inseam was PERFECT with heels from 2 inches to 3.75 inches. They are totally slimming, the rise is PERFECT (no digging if you know what I mean), the waistband is ULTRA COMFORTABLE and I have gotten compliments all over from my husband (the biggest fan) to ladies out in the stores, restaurants etc. Being a short person having a high-waist pant does help lengthen those short legs and I felt like a mini-super model with my extra long mini-legs! Do not hesistate to buy, and if you did like me and did the Try Before You Buy feature, you will definitely NOT be returning them like me and putting more in your cart. 10 STARS ALL AROUND. (Currently on "sale" at Dillards for $69.50). Get them here while they last!

Nice white stretchy pants I’m not sure about these pants. I like the bright whiteness of them and I like that there’s pockets. They’re definitely a yoga style pant however, I feel like you could wear them as leggings if you want to. My issue with them is that they’re not as thick as I would of hoped to have had them, now they are not see-through however, because they’re not that thick they don’t do everything you want, leggings or yoga pants to do. Hide “things”! It’s hard to tell in these pictures with great lighting(I am using a ring light), but they’re not hiding everything you would want to hide. Ss a matter of fact, in some bad lighting, or maybe even a little bit of shadowing, it kind of makes your legs look even worse than what they probably are. So if you have dimples or any kind of cottage cheese going on in the thigh/hamstring/glutes area, you’re not gonna wanna wear these by themselves, ladies you know what I mean. But I feel they would be a good layering pant. Or perfect for a young lady that has nice smooth legs or super fit no jiggle or sagging in the skin yet hahah! They are definitely stretchy, so that’s a plus. I am 5‘9“ 145 to 150 pounds and these are a size medium.

I am 5’6” and 130lbs. I was able to walk my normal strides and pace comfortably. The skirt did not place any restrictions to my legs walking. The buttons are all functional, you can undo as many as you want or none at all. Overall, I wanted a nostalgic fashion piece, and this Jean skirt was it. My teen daughters both really liked it on me and want to borrow it themselves. Despite the delivery time issues, it was an okay purchase over all. But I wouldn’t buy from this company again.

Looks and fits just like picture! LOVE! This denim skirt is SUPER cute! It's stretchy, it fits perfect around the waist, hugs the hips. It just doesn't cup under the butt like I would like lol if you get what I'm saying. I got a size MEDIUM. I'm 5'3" 145lbs.

favorite BUT THEY SHRANK So comfortable! Thick material. Stretchy waistband without buttons. They feel like thick luxurious lounge pants but they look perfect for the office. I worried about them making me look wide since I am short and size 12, but they are the perfect amount of wide. I’m 5’2” and ordered the short. They are perfect to wear with a heel. They just barely touch the floor without heels (see photo) so not the best to wear with flats on a rainy day. But if you are 5’2”, even the tiniest amount of heel or platform will work. Be careful how you care for them bc they shrank for me

Length shrinks A LOT I am not a tall person by any stretch of the imagination -- I'm 5'3" on a good day. I ALWAYS have to order short/petite pants or hem up regular pants by several inches. Having seen the reviews on these saying that they shrink, I went ahead and bought the regular length ones. Goodness, am I happy I erred on the side of caution. They started with a 29.5" inseam (see photo). After two or three wash and dries, they've shrunk 2" up to a 27.5" inseam. They're nearly too short for even me, but they'll work. That aside, they fabric is thick, soft, and high quality. It's perfect for work, lays flat, doesn't wrinkle, and it gives a nice silhouette for curvy hips/bum. They're also pull-ons for extra comfort, and they have front pockets. I really do like them and I'll be getting several more pairs for work, but I feel bad for anyone taller than me.

Nice, but not for my body type These pants are the “pull-on formal” type with no fly/ button, a thick elastic waist and detailing like overstitched pockets and belt hooks to make them work-appropriate. The fabric is of pretty decent thickness but if I kept them I’d probably need shapewear to cover the vpl and lumps and bumps. I ordered them in long, as the current fashion for flood pants doesn’t suit me, and they would be just about right with a modest heel (I’m 5’6” and somewhat short waisted). I have a small waist and big booty, but I was hoping they would work for me due to the stretch and elastic waist but they gaped really badly at the back. If I went down a size they would probably fit better at the waist but I wouldn’t want them to be any tighter on the hips for work pants. I returned them because of this but think the quality and style are good.

Very comfortable! Excellent quality Not sure why these pants got low reviews. But these Lee pants are legit comfortable. My measurements: waist 36 inch, hip 44 inch. Weight: 170, height: 5’6” I opted for the size 14 regular. I’m Somewhat curvier in the back. I did order 16, but it was too big, especially around the waist. The 14 is not too tight, or too loose. Inseam is perfect for my height. These are pull on with no zipper to deal with. Two side pockets that are deep. Back pockets are fake (cannot access). Material is a lil on the heavier/thicker side but soft and quality. Good stretch throughout. Try it out! I ended ordering more colors after this one.

Ok, but not perfect These were essentially what I was looking for, a lightweight, comfy, denim pull on but not quite a home run. FABRIC and STRETCH The fabric is NOT stretchy, only the waistband has stretch. Of course there will be color variation, but the pics show a more weathered, distressed look than the mine. SIZING and FIT: I tried both XL and 2X and there's not a whole lot of difference between the two as far as I can tell, but there's more room in the hip and thigh in the 2X. The fit isn't super flattering, especially from the back, but that could be because I have a relatively big, round, bum so it isn't as gathered back there as it is in the model pics. I think I'd probably need a 3X (which they don't have) to get that cute extra baggy look. OVERALL: For reference, I usually wear about a size 16 in pants. I'm plus sized (50-44-48) with a large bust and bum but no hips. I carry most of my weight in my belly. So while these will fit a plus sized body, they fit more like a regular relaxed jean than a baggy/slouchy jean. If I keep them, I will probably go with the smaller size (XL) because they're slightly more flattering as a relaxed jean, but if I could, I'd prefer to size up to get the slouchy, oversized look like the model.

Mixed Emotions OK, let me say right off that these are not flattering in any way. If you are single and wish to marry, I don’t advise wearing these around town—but they are really cute and comfortable! On the one hand, they make me look like I am wearing an adult diaper, but on the other they are so comfortable. I don’t know what to do. I will post some awful pics of myself wearing them so you will be sufficiently warned. That said, they are HUGE. I wish they came in extra small. Or XXS. I am 5’9” and 133 lbs. I usually wear a size 2 but these pants add about twenty pounds to my figure. See for yourself.

For reference I'm 5'1 with a butt and normally wear a size 5 in jeans or size small in sweats. Other than that, they seem good quality and the embroidery is flawless.

WAAAAAY TO SMALL!!!!! I purchased a Medium because the last belt I bought on Amazon was a medium and it fit perfectly . My waist is a 36 , and this belt is waaay to small . Fits perfect, and is as described! It fits as expected and is cute, I will be getting this in brown! For reference I am 5ft 3in 120lbs and typically wear a 26 in jeans. I got the size small belt.

Not for me.

Port is the new neutral This dark port color is a lot more versatile than you might think. I love this for a neutral alternative to black, brown or grey. It adds a pop of color without competing with the rest of your outfit. The design is a straightforward classic with a plain finish to the good quality leather. It's relatively sturdy but pliable and softens with wear. I anticipate it will last a good long while. I like the bonded finish on the edges too. the squarish buckle is like a tarnished brass for a lived in look. The tail end is squared with subtly rounded corners. This covers up the embossed Timberland logo when wearing. The medium size I ordered fits me well. I have a 31" waist and 41" hips and I can wear this on the second hole with midrise jeans, giving the belt a measurement of 34". There are five holes in this belt at one inch intervals measuring a span of 31" to 35" from tang to hole. The width is neither wide, nor skinny — 7/8" or 22mm, very workable and will fit through most belt carriers. I love it. Excellent quality with great workmanship. I wear this often!

It works After buying brown timberland boots, I wanted to get a matching belt. I usually wear a small in belts. I am 5'5 and 105lbs. Based on all the reviews claiming the sizing is super small, and to order two sizes up, I first ordered a large. That was a mistake, it was way too big. I exchanged for a medium, and that one fit better, but I really could've gone with a small, but I didn't want to return it again. I would say to order 1 size up to be safe. But if you're a shapeless pole like me, you may not have to.

These jeans are very comfortable! They are a stretchy, lightweight denim. I was worried I had ordered too small (I ordered size 6, 140 lbs, 8 months postpartum) and they fit great! The stretch really allows a lot of flexibility in the waist. The back with no pockets is very flattering. They do fit lengthwise with flats. The pictures had me thinking they were long and to be worn with heels, but I definitely don’t have any extra length for anything other then flats or sneakers. A good buy if you’re wanting comfy, not great if you want a thick denim or something that will provide some tummy control.

Very comfy. True to size I'm 5'6"and 174lbs I got a size large and they fit perfectly. If you prefer them to be longer they are not. But they aren't high waters so I'm on with it.

Get them! These jeans are the perfect fit! Sizing, color, material and texture are A1. I received so many compliments and they are very unique. Must buy, trust me. This is a size 14 and I’m 5’4” 199 pounds and petite. You’re welcome.

Different and fun jeans These jeans are a different design with the barrel legs, which make them a fun pair to wear. I love the pockets even if the front pockets aren't that deep; the ones in back fit my phone perfectly, so the front will work for my car key and my lip gloss. I like the way they fit, and I appreciate that they are a soft rather than stiff cotton, so they're comfortable against my skin. I usually wear my t-shirts out, but for purposes of the photographs, I tucked mine in front for this review. I am 5"5-1/2" tall, and you can see where the bottom of the pants hit me just right at the ankle. I'm glad I got these jeans!

These are capris... and I'm short! ✅Likes: ○ The color/wash is not too bad. Although I hate the fit of these (more to follow) but the wash is better than some other barrel cheap jeans I've gotten. No weird yellow tone or corny/exaggerated y2k fades. Kind of leans acid wash though so I don't like that as much though, but at least it's pretty even-toned. ❌Dislikes: ● Insanely short. If you're looking for the "barrel jeans via CAPRI" these may be up your alley, more gen-x/millenial does trendy than the ACTUAL barrel jean trend which bows out more and is BAGGY+full length. And no, I'm not tall. In fact, I'm barely average, a mere ~5'2" with short legs so... My previous go-to barrel jeans are a flat 26.5" in all sizes and that's MUCH better IMO... I ordered a 6 but tbh if I knew these were supposed to fit capri length I would try a maximum size 14 just so they sat lower (my reg size is M 26" waist, 39" hips) ● Not just short/capri style but they are AT LEAST AN INCH SHORTER THAN THE CHART so all I can say is size up IMO! 0.5-0.7" variation was incorrect in my case, tba if it's an accident but I think you should expect 1"+ so size up. ● Cheap construction, SUPER cheap zipper, 4 odd thick felt patches inside, super tight button hole, thick (but hollow) button, uncomfortable plastic thread ends everywhere, slippery synthetic pockets, etc ● Says "non stretch" yet there is spandex so yeah, they're somewhat stretchy and elastic will wear down

Inconsistent Size Chart I’m 5’7”, 129 lbs, 29” waist, 38” at the widest part of my booty. Size charts always seem to be so misleading. I can never rely on them. I read reviews trying to figure out what size to order. Several reviews said the size chart was pretty accurate, which meant I would have needed to order a large. I’m usually a small, but in considering the reviews I ordered a large and a medium and figured I’d return whichever didn’t fit. I ended up ordering a small after they arrive because the medium was too big. In the photos, I’m wearing a small, and you can see the pants could easily fit someone 2-3 inches wider than I am. They stretch big time. I’ll probably keep the small but not sure because the length is right at where I would want it to be and after washing/drying, it seems like this fabric would shrink quite a bit and then be too short. Either way, I would order a size down or at the very least your regular size when ordering these.

I suspect these won’t hold their shape over time and jeans this stretchy tend to accentuate my belly vs slimming it. Cute from the front, not as much from the side. The fabric is a bit rough and scratchy on my skin. The belt is super cute. These just touched the floor with bare feet, but became too short with a sneaker on. Because of that I would only wear these with flip flops and that’s just not versatile enough to add this piece to my wardrobe. Will be returning. Bummer :(

Huge Sizing Issue These pants are so beautiful (in photo and in person) and I was so excited to get them. I realize I am very thin and I am not as curvy as the model in the picture, but I am also not emaciated. I ordered the smallest size available, a small, and was thinking they may be a little loose…possibly. I am swimming in them and I’d have to gain 15-20 pounds to fill them out. They are also super long as you can see I’m barefoot in some of the pictures, and I actually wore 5 1/2 inch platform heels in another to show how they look. Which was fine in the heels. But I cannot believe how large these are for a small. I would have to guess these are about a size 28 in US sizing or a size 7/8. To me, these are a generous medium (maybe even large) and I wonder if size tags got mixed up. I’m 5f4 and weigh 106. I am thin, so I typically wear a size 0 jeans. However, I was not expecting these to be this big. Again, maybe a little loose, but wow! I am very disappointed because these jeans are beautiful. I can tell that they would look great if they made an extra (double extra) small.

Tight on the thighs and major waist gap This doesn’t fit like my other Carhartt pants. I have the same pants in a denim blue and they are perfect. These, in contrast, are a terrible fit. Awesome pair of jeggings! Runs a bit large. Based on other buyer reviews, I decided to purchase these in a size large. I'm 5'6 and 170 lbs. Typically I wear jeans that have a low rise because they're a better fit for my body type (extra weight in the tummy area.) High and mid-rise pants tend to cut into my belly fat and it creates these very unsightly lumps on my sides. Getting to the point, I wish I had gone with the medium instead of the large. They're form fitting but in a looser way. I like how they fit throughout the legs but I would prefer them a tad more fitting. Most jeggings (even the super stretchy ones) are too tight in the calves and ankles for me to wear comfortably. These are nice, smooth denim and very, very stretchy. They're perfect for someone like me who usually has trouble fitting into jeggings. There is a super comfy waistband that is equally stretchy (FYI: no belt loops or front pockets on these pants.) It's a higher waist but they're really easy to roll down, which is what I prefer. Even with them rolled down the waistband is still very big on me. I haven't washed them yet so I really hope they shrink some. If I were judging these on comfort level alone they would absolutely get 5 stars! They are literally just as comfortable as cotton leggings. The denim is very soft, not thick and rough like traditional jeans. I'm definitely ordering another pair in a smaller size! Highly recommend!!

Really great belt! No bulk! I am a short person (4’11) and have a short torso on top of that. Not to mention I am also currently a bit overweight and carry it in my midsection from previous pregnancies. Therefore, when I wear jeans that require a belt I end having to hike them up above my belly button and my belt bulge looks ridiculous under my shirt and makes me feel uncomfortable and more self conscious. I saw this product and thought I would give it a try. It’s really neat because it is stretchy and adjustable so it is able to fit a variety of different sizes with just one belt. That way as I lose weight I can just tighten it up, rather than having to get a completely new one. One downside to the elastic type material is that when you undo your belt it tends to “snap” back and it will come out of at least one of my belt loops. This is a bit annoying when I’m trying to go to the restroom and attempting to keep my belt from coming out of my loops all together. Lastly, the buckle itself can be a bit tricky. It does have a way about it to get it just right. It took me several times until I finally figured out the placement of pressure to get it to release easier. And sometimes it releases when I don’t quite want it to if it’s being held at a certain angle. But, at the end of the day this is still an awesome alternative to your typical belt. It’s also super comfortable to wear. Most belts are painful for me to keep on when sitting or driving, but I haven’t had that issue with this. I definitely recommend.

Ideal for tall women One of the appeals to this style is the wide legs. For women like me, with rather thick thighs, these pants are liberating and offer much comfort. These did not disappoint in that aspect. But overall, the length was a huge drawback (reduce a star). They were like 5 inches too long. I attempted to roll the waist band to reduce the length but they were still too long and doing so caused the pants to bunch in the rear end. I attempted to roll them from the bottom at no avail; the cuff would not stay. Nevertheless, the feel was super soft with rather shallow pockets for such a relaxed style (reduce a star).I did order my normal size 12. For reference, I am 5'6" at 175lbs with a waist of 35". I felt like the elastic would help to frame my waist, but I wish I had ordered a smaller size because I did not feel any curve was sightly in these pants.

Size chart true for US sizing. Super soft and cute! For comparison: I am 5’5” and I am usually 120 lbs but post holiday I’m rocking 125-130 lbs. I normally wear US size 4 or 27 in pants and size medium bikini bottoms. I have wide hips, some junk in the trunk, and shorter legs. Product: With the extra winter weight hanging on tight, mostly due to my love of chocolate and avoidance of the outdoors when the temperature is under 60, I decided to order medium Grapent jeans. I did not want the pants to be too tight and hinder my couch potato winter lifestyle. That was a mistake. The provided size chart recommended a size small but I have trust issues when it comes to size charts so I went up a size. Not only are the jeans plenty big in the waist, maybe for 8/10/12, but there is quite a bit of stretch in the material. Also, the fabric lost a bit of shape after a day of running errands. I fully regret not getting the recommended small because the material is very soft and stretchy so I think I would love them if they fit better. That’s my bad! The fabric is not a true denim but rather a medium weight cotton/poly blend. There are functional hip pockets as well as back pockets. I especially like the thigh rip because it is a different style than my other ripped jeans. As for length, I had to roll the cuffs to wear with sneakers. I’m normally a 29” inseam and these jeans were probably 30-31”. These waist just qualifies as “high” landing just past my belly button. The rear area was the only fitted part with the legs being looser. Recommendation: These mom jeans are stylish and super comfortable. I recommend going by the size chart in this case and rest easy knowing the fabric has plenty of stretch. The only negative thing I found was the tag instructs to hand wash while the product page advertises machine washable. I am going to machine wash but hang to dry and hope for the best. I really like these jeans and would recommend to a friend!

I LOVE THESE! They are so soft. I didn't realize how stretchy they would be (should've read the reviews: big green checkmark by "stretchiness"). I'm in a fluffy season.
