Love these! These are great jeans if you're a curvy girl like me. If you are hourglass or pear shaped... these jeans check all the boxes....relaxed fit in the hip and thighs and super stretchy. I'm 5'4 and the length is about perfect for me. The funny thing is they are advertised as having a rise of 9.5" (mid rise), however I knew that couldn't be true because the waistband came up above my belly button. So I measured the rise and it was 12"! I wasn't mad about it because I love high rise jeans. The only negative I could find about these jeans is they are quite a thin denim material and I question how long they will last, especially after seeing some of the other reviews. But for the low price I paid I'm sure I'll get my money's worth.

What a funny joke to call these xxs These are huge! The size they sent was indeed labeled xxs...but what the heck?! I fit the given measurements, but they must all be totally wrong. These are falling off, as you can see I have to hold them on my actual waist where they are supposed to sit. They are nice, but just comically large, I am short, only 4"11, but the rest of my measurements are the same size as the size chart. Also, you can see the white interior of the pockets through the pants, the fabric is not thin but still see through, so dark or white underwear or colored tucked in shirts are a no. They are more of a cream which is what I wanted and expected from the pictures. For the price, not worth it, I've gotten better and more accurately sized things on shein. Also even with heels, the pants are dragging the ground, so def not for short people. But idek, if I could have gone any smaller than an XXS, at this rate, it probably would have had to be an xxxxxs🙄 I wouldnt trust this sizing at all.

Order down one size from your usual jeans - they will loosen over time I wear size 6-8 in jeans. I first ordered these in size 8 and loved the fit and the faded whisker-look along the top. After walking around in them for a few minutes, I felt them sliding down a bit. We all know that jeans will continue to loosen throughout the day so I opted to exchange for size 6, which is what I typically wear in KUT from the Kloth corduroys. The fit was great though the whisker fade was much less prominent in the newly exchanged pair (dang it!) The photo I've included is of the size 8 jeans. I like the mid-rise fit. It feels secure and flattering around my mid section but still hip and stylish. I also like the ripped look but not so ripped that I feel like a teenager or homeless person. I have long legs (34 inseam) and these cropped in the perfect place on my calves. Love them!

Get the large, even if you're thin I love these leggings. They are super soft and fleece lined (which is so comfortable). I am 5'2" and 117 lbs (29-30 inch waist) so I bought two pairs in large/xl based on my waist (shown in the photo attached) and I own a pair in small (which is what I originally purchased). Honestly, you would not see a difference in a photo of me wearing either the small or the large size. The large is a little longer in the legs and comes up above my bellybutton, but other than that I don't feel much of a difference. It definitely feels more comfortable pulling it up so it aligns with the top of my legs to look like jeans rather than tights (and I'm short!). I will likely purchase it again in the large size since there's not much of a difference to me in tightness. Although I could see this stretching to fit a heavier person, I wouldn't consider it xl sized. I usually wear small or medium in other stores, and I'd consider this more of a medium size.

Good for the Price I bought these in size S/M but I think I could've gone with a Large very easily. I'm normally a size 6/8 and small in women's clothes, medium in anything"trendy" (like forever 21, H&M, etc.). I'm 5'8”, measurements 34-24-38, and most pants are a little short on me, these were no exception. I was expecting to probably have to exchange these for the larger size, especially right after I took them out of the package. They look like clothes for a ten year old, but these leggings stretch an absurd amount. I would not suggest a S/M for someone who has any extra "love-lumps" going on because I feel like these will role down in that situation. They're high waisted enough to feel really comfortable and they're not the warmest leggings but it's not like I can feel a breeze when I'm wearing these (like some other leggings I have, looking at you Target). Would buy again. My biggest suggestion is that, if you are trim and slim, buy the S/M. If you are anywhere else on the spectrum, buy the Large.

Very odd fabric and printing I ordered these in a size L/XL, and normally wear a size 10-12. It fit fine with no muffin top. However, the fabric is a silky feel, shiny, lightweight material on which the denim markings are printed. I knew about the printing from other reviews but did not expect the fabric to be so shiny in a denim print. The whiskering, fading and fly/pocket/contrast stitch printing all look so incredibly fake hah. It was also pretty short on my ankles (I normally wear an inseam 30 in skinny jeans and 31.5 in straight leg). Overall it looked kind of ridiculous on me. I still gave it 3 stars though because my expectations were different, but others may be looking for something exactly like this. I've uploaded a couple of pictures including closeups of the print.

Love these!! I freaking love these stretch pants. They truly look like jeans but wear like a second skin. In my opinion these are spring/fall type stretch pants. Too hot for summer but wont keep you warm in winter. They are kinda long (I'm 5'4" about 170lbs) and they bunch at my ankles and sit high in my waist. BUT- 1. They're perfect to wear with calf boots!! 2. If you want to wear flipfloos or sandals...they easily fold up to look like a cuff. And 3. Even though they are high on my waist, there's no seem or zipper bulge; so no funky lines under a t-shirt. But best of all, it doesnt matter what color undies I have on. I can bend over and it doesnt stretch so much that my undies peak thru the fabric. Worry free wear! (And I have hips and a booty, so they definitely have to stretch!- lol). I would recommend.

I’m adding this info for future buyers because I myself like to read a lot of reviews before buying clothes lol.I’m 5’7” and fluctuate between 145lbs - 150lbs. I bought a size 8 (regular length). I’ve recently gained weight so I don’t know my true size right now, I just ordered one size up from what I used to be. I still fit in Free People size 28 jeans, if that’s of any help to anyone.These jeans fit comfortably enough BUT they’re also pretty snug so if they shrink in the dryer they probably won’t fit me anymore. I honestly think I could’ve gone with a size 10 in case they do end up shrinking.. I’m still considering exchanging them. I haven’t washed or worn them out yet, so I don’t know how well they’ll hold up. I will say that the material seems to be thicker and better quality than I expected. They have some stretch but not enough to be considered that weak jegging material (I don’t like), they actually feel like jeans. I also like the length, they’re not short and not too long, as you can see in the picture (I’m not wearing shoes). As for the rise of the waist, I'd say it's mid-rise because it sits about an inch below my belly button. They're definitely not low-rise on me, which I'm glad about because I hate low-rise.All in all, they seem like a pretty good pair of jeans, and if my first impression is correct, they’ll be sturdy and last a decent while.I hope my review helped someone out!

Comfort, Good Fit for Petite This is my first time ordering jeans online because I can never seem to find ones that fit me even at the store. I'm 5 2", 120 pounds, and ordered a size: 8 Petite. The attached photo is how they fit. It's pretty good, except I had to fold in the hem (so I will be fixing that myself, yup! even in petite size), but that is a minor thing) the rest of the fit is good. They are comfortable. I'm not really having the same issue as others with the low-back, though they are a low-rise for sure. Mine lay about an inch below my belly button. I'm comfortable with that. If you're on the edge, definitely worth trying these since they're easy to return.

Unique legging design, with jean pockets and a slightly flared bell bottom. Moderately flared bell bottom, high waisted jean leggings with side and back pockets. The fabric is a blend of 57% cotton, 29% polyester and 14% spandex with a slightly distressed horizontal pattern that runs across the front of these pants in the pelvic area, as if to mimic natural creasing. They are generally stretchy, with a comfortable waist design that allows for ease of movement and good leg length on a 5'6" frame. The inseam on the left leg does rotate toward the front once past the knee; however, these pants are a dark charcoal color and with the flare at the bottom it is less obvious than it would be on a straight leg pant. The fabric is heavy enough to hold its shape and also provide some warmth for cool weather wear. I did run them in the dryer until just damp without any apparent shrinkage, although the label does advise a cool wash and line dry.

Flattering Denim Pants, Excellent Fit. I was surprised these “jeggings” are actually real denim and have functional pockets. The only thing that doesn’t qualify them as jeans is a zipper, I suppose. I’m 115lbs, 5’7”, 27”Waist, 35”Hips, 18”Thighs, and got an XS. I have zero curves, but these make it look like I might actually have hips. For once, jeans that hug my skinny thighs instead of being baggy. Normally I would get a Small, but I’m happy I sized down. The length is perfect -hitting just above the floor. The waistband is really tight though, I almost couldn’t pull them up. But it’s made of the same denim the rest of the pants are, which I prefer over a separate stretcher material. No belt-loops though. Overall, very flattering and comfortable denim pants.

Good fit and comfy cargo jeans - non-thin material This review is for: QICAMO Women High Waisted Cargo Jean for Women… (Dark Blue, 4)QICAMO Women High Waisted Cargo Jean for Women… (Dark Blue, 4) Listed at $46 these cargo pants are not the thin cheap material kind, and they’re better quality than expected. I found the material. Quite comfortable as they’re a cotton mostly plus some spandex blend. The size 4 fit the 120lbs 5’4” person perfectly well based on the size chart they provided. My only issue is that the pockets on the sides look a bit awkward bc they’re different from each other and they don’t have buttons nor clips so the flap needs to be ironed to look good after washing so it can keep its shape. I think $46 is kind of expensive for them imho, but it’s kinda cool to have high rise to cover the food belly and still have plenty of pockets for putting things away.

In style and so comfy! These jeans are a very cute with an on trend style. They have cargo pockets on the legs, plus the usual 2 front pockets and 2 back pockets which are all roomy. They are high waisted, baggy and wide legged at the ankles. The black color is a deep rich black - blacker than my photos which came out lighter. These jeans have stretch making them super comfortable. The length is past the ankle to just touch the ground on my 5’3” frame. The tag says to not put them in the dryer, but I did and they are fine. I imagine they will fade quicker if I keep tumble drying them, but I always use low heat. I don’t like air drying jeans. I am happy with these jeans!

Forever customer I’m 5’3, 265lbs. I love these! The fabric looks more like denim than any other legging/jegging I’ve tried. They have just the right amount of stretch, they aren’t too thin or too thick, and I’ve washed them a half dozen times and so far they’ve held their shape. I love that the waistband is slightly wide, but still thin and flexible. I’ve had multiple abdominal surgeries so I don’t like really thick waistbands or any kind of hardware digging in. With me being shorter, they obviously aren’t quite capris, but I really love these and I hope they keep making them forever because I will buy them till the day I die , lol.

Great jeggings! Will definitely buy more! Being a larger lady I have always hidden myself behind baggy tops and pants. Someone advised me to try cropped jeggings for a change and I’m so glad I did. These are lovely! They look and feel like proper jeans (not just like denim-look fabric) but are so stretchy and comfortable. They are high waisted which I like, and they offer quite a bit of control even though they are extremely comfy. I’m 5’ 5 and weigh 205lbs and the XL fit perfectly. The length is exactly what I wanted - approx 6” above the ankle. I love them so much I ordered another pair within 30 minutes of putting them on!

Worst fit ever I wasn't expecting top quality for the price, but these were so awful in so many ways that I don't think they're worth $1. The material feels very cheap and there isn't much stretch to the fabric. There's also these very odd puckers of extra fabric on the outside of the hips and on the inseam in the crotch area, which looks ridiculous. I'm 5'4", 140lbs and got the large size capris because I wanted a looser fit, but these were slightly snug in the waist and loose around the thighs, especially with that extra pucker of fabric. Also, these were not capri length on me. I have short legs and a long torso and, as you can see, they go down almost to my ankles. I'd have to roll them up many times to get the length I want, but the fabric isn't stretchy enough and is too snug around the lower legs to do this. Finally, there are back pockets, but the front pockets are fake. And be mindful of the tag that warns about bleeding dye even after washing. Given all of these faults, I wouldn't recommend these to anyone. There's really nothing positive to say about them. Don't waste your money.

Tons of stretch I am 5' 10'' tall, I weigh 175lbs and ordered an XL...fits great. I typically wear size 16-18 up in pants but closer to 16. I love how the legs are pinned up to show the fray at the bottom. But if you don't like them pinned up you can take them down easily. The bottom of the pants are pinned up by only a couple/few stitches in four places on each leg. These overalls have five pockets-two roomy pockets on each side in the front, two smaller pockets on the back and one large pocket (shaped like a back pocket) on the front. And did I mention these overalls have STRETCH? Matter of fact, there are four buttons on each side to undo to assist in putting these on but with so much stretch in this fabric I didn't need to undo even one button. Although these overalls don't look quite as sexy on me as they do the model, I do like them. And I plan on getting a lot of wear out of them.

Spacious, Sturdy, and Super Cute! This jean jacket is the first one I’ve had that fits me well in many, many years, and I am so happy I ordered it. I am 5’7” and normally wear a size 16/XL, and this XL is spot-on. It is spacious, like I could wear it over a sweater on an autumn day, but not too big. I like that I can button it and it’s not snug through my mid-section. It has a nice length to cover the waist and hips, with small side slits for flexibility, too. The denim material is sturdy and well-stitched, making me think it will last a long while. Even if the jacket shrinks a bit after the first washing, I am confident it will still fit, so overall, I give this super cute denim jacket a five-star rating.

Good Value For Quality Slimming Jean There's nothing more timeless than a good pair of basic blue jeans. The Dickies Women's Perfect Shape Denim Jean-Straight Stretch lives up to its name and offers tremendous value for well made durable pants that move with you while staying in place. These Dickies are the type of jean that you'll look forward to wearing if an effortless style and fit are what you're after. They fit loosely without slippage and flatter the form with its straightforward sleek design. My wife wears size 8-10 and the 10RG fits her 5'5 ½" frame just fine, albeit a little loosely around the crotch and derrière. That's fine with her given the comfort it provides and the classic style it affords. The stitching is solid and the quality button and zipper are signature Dickies. The material feels durable yet it's not too thick or bulky. The stretchy material infused in the cotton fabric perfectly accommodates an active lifestyle. The pockets are large and practical and can be used to store personal effects like wallet or phone. The length is versatile, working with both flats and heels. My wife loves boots, so these work well as a boot cut with it's stretchy design. But as the Florida weather warms up, sandals will be her go-to footwear and these look great with open-toe shoes. The Jean-Straight Stretch stretches the form and provides a slimming effect that works great if you choose to tuck in a shirt, but it's form fitting enough for untucked longer lengths as well. ~ Kort

Shocked at how much I like these I had super low expectations for random brand Amazon jeans but wow, I am impressed! They have their flaws for sure, but they are WAYYYY better than expected and super comfortable. Some thoughts: - Run a bit large. I am 5'7" and 140lbs. I usually wear a 27/28 waist but went down to a 27 because they are oversized and I didn't want them TOO large. The fit is PERFECT, however, they are tighter on my legs than shown in the photos, I wonder if I had went with a 28 if they would fit more "boyfriend" like. - Unbelievably comfortable. They are so stretchy and so soft but they maintain their shape. - A bit shorter on me than the model. Again, I am 5'7" and they fit above my ankle. - Rips all look cute and are well placed - Butt pockets.....thats the biggest yuck. They are placed poorly for me and are not flattering on the booty. This is the reason I dropped them a star! Overall, I have already ordered another style from this brand! They're so comfy and cute! Very very happy! Definitely consider sizing down if you between sizes though!

Great! I really like these jeans! I'm 5'2", 120lbs, 28" waist/34" hip and ordered a size 27 in Blue-4. The lay-flat measurements are as follow: 13.5" waist, 10.5" rise, 25" inseam (so pretty close to what the size chart says). They're made of a soft, stretchy material - I usually prefer non-stretch denim, but I like these because they don't feel thin or cheap, and they're sturdy enough to hold their shape. The online photos make them look like they're made of all cotton and have a button fly, though neither are true in real life. However, I still found them to be very comfortable and flattering, and they're not too distressed, which I like. They're more of a skinny/straight leg fit than I expected, but the waist fits perfectly so I don't think they're too small. All in all, I think they're a very versatile style of jeans, and I'll enjoy wearing them. Please note: be careful when washing these. I threw them in the dryer in hopes that they'd shrink a little bit and get a little tighter, but they shrank vertically instead! I lost at least half an inch off the length of the legs. Not a big deal when you're short like me, but someone taller might have an issue with it.

Nicer than expected These Lee pull ons are a lot nicer than I expected. They are a good fit with some give and don't seem to be the type of jeans that stretch out after 10 minutes. I put these on out of the package and wore them for about 30 minutes around the house bending and stooping, and they held their shape. I washed them per the instructions then dried them and they did shrink a bit in the length, so be mindful. This was a welcomed surprise, because they were a little long. Now they're perfect. I am 5'3" and 145-150 pounds with curves and a belly. I feel like they're flattering to my shape. I'd buy again.***Update*** These jeans really didn't shrink that much in the length, less than I originally thought. I usually wear a bit of heel or tennis shoes with these. Even so, I love these jeans. They still look and fit great.

Cuffs on sleeves are tight… Lets dive in, The dress itself has a very loose fitting. So I added a belt to give it some shape. The buttons work perfectly, and they align evenly. Beautiful denim color. Dress, without a belt fits right above the knees for reference, I am 5’4. Overall a fun piece to add to your wardrobe.

Great accent pieces I’m surprised by the quality of these belts. They are well made for the price point, and the simplicity of the buckle elevates any outfit. The belts were packaged with care. Each belt had a sleeve and the buckles had protective tape on them. They also came in a nice box. I got the large size and I am 170 lbs and carry weight around my belly. When I use the belt on my pants, I could button it on the 3rd to last notch. When I use it for an accent piece with my dresses, I can get it to the last notch because it sits higher on my waist. I could go for a smaller size, but I don’t like the look of the extra holes showing on the belt. With the larger size, most of the notches are hidden and makes the belt look more tailored and streamlined. The extra length of the belt that goes around the waist underneath doesn’t give much bulk and is still comfortable. So it’s up to personal preference whether you want to go with size or look. Both the brown and black are great colors to work with any outfit. Whether to pull the look together or to break up sections. I’d purchase it again in a different color.

I never say this but I love the bell bottoms. I don't like bell bottoms on me because they somehow make me look, short, stumpy, and is historically a shape that is not so flattering on my body. These are however different. I love them. It's probably because they are cropped. I've gone through life staying away from that shape but these jeans changed it for me. The Jean material itself has a nice thickness and stretch to it. There is an odor and a chemical smell that's gives me skin reactions, so I will not be wearing these until I put them in 2 very hot machine washes. I ordered in a size 2 and it fits perfectly. For reference, I am 5ft 2 in, 120-125 lbs, athletic build, and size small/x-small, 0-2, 25-26 inch jeans fit me best. So, do order true to size. All the stitching and sewing was done well with no open seams, overlapping of material or loose hanging thread. Get these jeans! They are so flattering on.

Halloween vibes These jeans are cute, but they look like pants to a sexy Frankenstein or Hulk costume. The faux distressed hem doesn't resemble normal wear and tear: it's too symmetrical. I'm going to have to either hem them and make capris or cut them to (ironically) look less deliberate. But the cotton/spandex blend makes the fabric stretchy, soft and comfortable, and the pants have five pockets (including the fob) and belt loops like regular jeans. They're a bit loose on me, so I'd have to say they run large. For reference, I'm 5'8", 120 lbs, 26 waist, 35 hip, 33 inseam (not relevant here) and ordered a 4. They're borderline low riders but don't have a short rise, which is good. In other words, not a Junior's fit, more women's (but not matronly).

Save your money and buy from trusted brands! I am nearly 5'11, 200 lbs. and am a size 14, which is a Large, but I ordered an XL based off reviews: It just literally arrived and I will be returning them after I send this review. I was disappointed upon seeing and feeling them, but to be certain I briefly tried them on- being extremely careful not to snag any of the overexagerated cuts with my toes. It fit well and had a good stretch too them, however the cuts aren't reinforced properly and on the right leg knee cut it was already dangling- no thank you! In my opinion these are way overpriced! If you are curvy and have thighs that rub- I can predict they will leave additional holes in the inner thigh area with normal use in minimal time- just my assumption based off the material. For the price- buy yourself some trusted Levi's, Lee's, Wranglers, Old Nay, Gap- even Lucky Brand (on clearance😜). If thinner, more slender, then, yeah, these can work if you're wanting extremely ripped up Jean's, and don't mind paying too much for them... your preference is your own.

Save your money and buy from trusted brands! Full. Size. Pockets!!! These destroyed jeans are amazing! The front pockets are full size so you can fit your entire cell phone in them. They're very on trend especially for about $35, the stretch is perfect and holds up. I'm 5'6" and 150 lbs and the medium fit perfectly.

I love these jeans! Comfort level = A+ First time ever that I have ordered a pair of jeans online. I have always been one to go and try them on in the store before buying, but with the stretch waistband and spandex in them, I decided to take a chance. I am 5'4" and 180-ish so I ordered the X-Large. I have a problem with so many in the pants line being too long due to my height. Figured with these it might work out to be just about right since they are supposed to be cropped. I was right! They are nearly a normal length on me, and I do not mind that it alters what the style was meant to be at all, lol. I dress for comfort, not fashion. These jeans are very soft and have a forgiving stretch that makes them super comfy to wear. I was surprised at how thick and heavy in weight they are. They will be nice and toasty warm when cooler weather gets here and in the winter. First pair of jeans too that I have ever been able to tuck in a shirt and did not have a spare tire hangover at the top of the jeans. These jeans are well made and look like they will last a long time. I can highly recommend these jeans. If I see a good sale on them, I will be grabbing a second pair, I like them that much! Very happy I took the chance on them.

Very comfy and high quality silky soft jean that feels like a legging. I am 5'7" and 160lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size M and L. I got this in a size L and they fit perfectly. These are very similar to the Halara Magic Denim jeans, they have that very silky soft legging feeling to them. They have great movement and feel cool and silky against your legs. The wide waistband is flattering and they have plenty of stretch, you can literally work out in these if you want with no issues.I love that they have decent sized hand pockets and butt pockets. These wash up beautifully without any shrinkage in the laundry.I have a couple pairs of the Halara Magic Demin pants and absolutely love them but have really been wanting a place to buy something similar that didn't come from Halara. These pants are perfect for that, they are exactly the same and just as amazing to wear.These are an excellent, lightweight, flattering, comfy jean and I can't recommend them enough. So excited to have a jean option like this to buy on amazon!

Comfy but front pockets make them poofy My stats: 5' tall, 114 pounds, 29 1/2 inch waist, 36 inch hips, 22 inch inseam; 60 years old, carry my weight in my abdomen and thighs. I purchased size small/short. I have to fold up the bottoms a couple of inches, which I usually have to do, so not a problem there. While they are comfortable and I wear them a lot, I am not totally satisfied with them and would try another brand/style rather than purchasing them again. On the plus side, they are very stretchy yet are heavy enough to look like jeans. The back pockets are nice. I washed them in cold water and dried on medium heat and they did not shrink on me. What I don't like: Mainly the front pockets, which are deep and they allow too much fabric in front of them, such that the abdomen area looks poofy and the thighs look wrinkly. I was hoping for something a little more flattering. While I do not wear nor want to wear them with a shirt tucked in, I am attaching photos of the pants with my shirt tucked in so you can see the waistline, which also feels a little too thick for my tastes. An additional negative: It gives me a slight camel toe, of which I am constantly aware, though it's not so bad that I won't wear them. I am also attaching a photo of me in a pair of pocketless exercise pants so you can see what I look like without pockets poofing out.

Long enough skinny jeans - FINALLY I'm 5'10" and 146 lbs. I got size Large Long and I'm super happy with the length. They even bunch just a little around my ankles which I love and can never seem to find, being tall. I don't have to constantly be pushing at them to keep them from riding up to short, the length is amazing. I love how the pants fit, they are flattering but not too tight. There is good give in them and they feel like real jeans, not leggings, but are still comfy. I have bigger hips and a small waist and there is just the slightest gap in the back where the pants don't hug the way I would like them to ideally, but I usually wear looser tops so it doesn't show at all, and its not so uncomfortable that I won't get used to it. I ordered the dark wash and they weren't exactly the color that is shown, and at first I was a little disappointed but the more I've worn the pants the more they are growing on me and I can see them becoming my go-tos.

Definitely warm! I am impressed with these jeans! The color is great, and the fit is size accurate according to the sizing chart. They definitely are high waisted. The pants have a nice but slight stretch. The inside of the legs are super soft and the pants definitely keep you nice and toasty. I definitely recommend if you are going to be outside in the cold, say, for a night football game. They may be a little too toasty in a crowded place with heat, unless you just are cold natured. The pants are an average length (they were long on me as I’m 4”11) but will pair nicely with boots. You can’t really roll them up at the ankle as the inside lining shows. I do wish they left a little room without lining so they can be rolled.

Almost Too Perfect. I don’t think anyone is loving the elastic band on these pants, but that is my only complaint. I like my jeans tight around my butt/thighs, which is hard because I don’t much have a butt or thighs. But I love this fit and they feel like wearing comfy sweatpants. I’m 5’7”, 115lbs, 27”Waist, 34”Hips, 18” Thighs and got an XS. There’s a bit of a loose area in front, but it doesn’t look weird and makes space for more curvy figures. The fleece does add a scotch of bulk, but I need all the bulk I can get on my skinny legs. The cut is tighter in the lower legs than the seat and thighs. Probably not great if you’ve got larger or athletic calves, but the stretch is fantastic. I put these in the dryer on low to tighten up just a little, no problems. The fleece isn’t terribly thick like another pair I have, but is still warm and super soft. The blue is a nice wash on the less faded side than some. I very much recommend these fleece jeans to pretty much anybody.

While they are a bit snug around the knee and calf area, it's not bothersome. These jeans pair wonderfully with various tops, coats, and boots, making them a great choice for colder weather. I am looking forward to wearing them when the temperatures drop. For reference, I am 5’ 3”, 119lb, and typically wear size 4 (XS or S). I opted for the S size, and it fits me well.

On the right is the Avery q-baby, on the left is the boot scootin q-baby. I am no stranger to wranglers or q baby and I quite enjoy the denim and love the fit. I purchased a size 11x30 and a 9x30 in the Avery to see what would fit better as I recently lost some weight. The 11 was too large in the waist so I exchanged it for a 9x30 in the boot scootin so I didn’t have two pairs of the same jeans. I started the return the day I received them. It seems it’s slim pickings for wrangler sizes, especially with a 30 inseam. The Avery length is more of a 32 inch inseam although the tag indicates 30 inch but they are a super comfortable and stretchy jean, they fit large for a size 9 in the waist. I am a true 30 inseam and will have to get the Avery’s hemmed. The boot scootin is the perfect length but slightly too tight in the waist and they are sold out of the 11x30 to exchange. The boot scootin also has some stretch but not near as much as the Avery. It’s quite frustrating to have such varying fits of q baby jeans. I will likely have to return the boot scootin jeans as they are just a little too uncomfortable to wear if I was to be sitting for a long period of time. Pretty bummed there aren’t more choices to pick from online.

If you're like me, it's a must have. Firstly, I usually don't create lengthy/detailed product reviews on Amazon --unless I feel as if my review may help someone. For starters, I'll describe myself for a more "personalized" review. I'm around 5'5", 140 lbs, with a small waist and thicker bottom half. My wardrobe consists mainly of mid to high waisted jeans/shorts. Needless to say, rarely do I find jeans/shorts that fit me perfectly in both the waist and bottom half. Most of the time, they'll fit well in one area OR the other. Additionally, I dislike everything to do with typical belts on me or with any outfit I wear. I dislike the look, the uncomfortable feeling of it, and especially the buckles. After purchasing some new jeans recently, I ran into my normal "awkward fit" problems. I thoroughly researched many clothing sites that I liked, and Amazon, for regular belts that I may like a little. After having no luck, I thought to myself, "People have created products to help those needing their pants to be larger in the waist, so surely, some genius must have invented a magical product, other than the normal belt, to solve the opposite problem." Then, I found this gem and decided to give it a try. I received it today, so this is my first time wearing it. I'll say, it is pretty magical. Not only does it hold my pants at my waist, but it's also discrete. My only complaints may be that it's not very fashionable and it tends to ride up a tad, but not a huge problem given that it's doing its job well and because I can hide it. I'll end with this statement: if you're like me and suffer from the same "awkward pants fitting" problems, try this out.

SIZE DOWN-RUNS BIG I love everything about these pants. The color is a beautiful deep burgundy. Material is soft. It has 4 real pockets. Length is perfect for someone of my height. I ordered a 6. For reference: I am 5.5 1/2 and 140lbs. I am currently in-between sizing. I usually wear a 7/8. Some 6's fit, but tend to be snug in the crotch area when sitting. So I figured it would be a safe bet to go with the 6 since they run big and they would not be snug and they are not snug at all. I probably could have ordered a 4 and would be completely fine. I may get the Royal Blue in a 4 and compare the fit of the two, but we will see. Overall I am very happy with these pants. I am giving it a 4 star rating b/c through out the day, they did tend to ride up a bit & are starting to bunch at the top of my boots, but that could be b/c these are big on me, comfortable, but big. I would definitely recommend getting these if you are on the marked for comfortable, burgundy pants.

I love high waisted tapered leg jeans, which these are. The problem is they are super stretchy and run way too big. I purchased the smallest size offered (size 2). I suppose their 00 would fit me ok. If you have some good hips and butt, I think these might run fairly true to size, but for someone with my figure, you'll definitely want to size down. They didn't show panty lines which is rare!

Stretchy material with a great fit! My daughter absolutely loves these pants! She’s 5’0”, 125lbs and she said, ”The material is stretchy and has a great fit Fits at the waist but loose on the rest, definitely like it as a short girl but should wear with shoes with a slight platform.”

Love them I love these way these feel and fit. The cotton is really soft, seams appear solid. The color is bright and they are just comfy. I really could lounge in them, feel like pjs. I'm 5ft 200lbs and bought a size large. Although I liked and wanted the relaxed feel, I could've purchased a small and would still comfortably fit in them. I have a lot of wiggle room and stretch, even in the waistband, which has a drawstring. I would knot the ends to prevent string from going in hole when washed. The waistband stretches another 5-6 inches out for me and my belly is 41 inches. I prefer the gathering at the ankles because tight fits around the ankle aren't flattering on me but with that being said a tall person can rock these maybe up to 5'5 or 5'6. I prefer the roominess of the large as it hides my fupa. I bought four designs and only have to return one as the red plaid has a hole in the right pocket. Oh, it has pockets! Not shallow but not deep enough to hold cell phone unless you had a tighter fit, like if I had gotten the small the phone would fit snug. They are not transparent so no worry about underwear showing. They are lightweight. I'll update the breathability later as I live in Florida and it gets hot. They have found a repeat customer. I'll update after I do my first wash to see how it holds up. Good value for the money.

Comfy Cozy Pants All Day Long I have these pants in 3 patterns, and I've bought more patterns for my friends too because I just love these comfy cozy pants! At 5'4" and 150lbs, the large size fits me comfortably. The medium was a little snug with less freedom of movement. The pockets are an okay size, not too shallow but definitely not very deep. The fabric material is sooo soft and has held up to being washed regularly. Since I've bought so many of them, I noticed the cut of the print is not the same each time, so you do run the risk of having a giant bullseye print right on your crotch or butt, or having the pattern repeat in a weird way also drawing attention to the crotch. So, I've had to return a few pairs as well for bad cuts of the patterns, or for having holes in the pockets or ankle cuffs. These pants are cheap, afterall. But I can't get enough of 'em!

Great Joggers! I absolutely loved these joggers as soon as I opened the package - as per previous reviews they are incredibly soft, the length was perfect. I ordered the grey camo print and the color was exactly as displayed on my monitor. I normally wear size 0-2 and ordered Medium in these pants as most of the reviews mentioned to size up; that didn't quite work for me - they were too baggy in the waist as well as hips most likely because I'm not that curvy (waist - 25" and hips - 36"). I am exchanging them for size Small - I will update the review when the new pair arrives. UPDATE: After receiving the new pair in size Small I can honestly say that these are my favorite joggers ever (I'm going to order more in different colors and prints and I won't break the bank doing so :)). The fit is great : not tight, not baggy, the fabric is thin, buttery soft and washes well (the pants kept their shape after a few washes). The waist band is very elastic and the drawstring is easily removable if that's your preference. The one thing I would mention about these pants is that on my frame (5'4") they sit above my belly button, which would make them high-waisted; if you're much taller than me, that may not be the case for you.

Thrilled I took a chance on this product. I was skeptical about less than 15 dollar pants, not gonna lie, but these are better than I hoped. Pardon the mess but I wanted to show the length and my room is a wreck. I’m 5’7 and 130 lbs and took a chance on size small, and they fit like a glove. Waistband feels secure but has some give, and length(which I was most worried about) is just right, resting on my ankle bone. There is a good bit of stretch too, so I can sit or lay however and they don’t feel stressed or pulled too tight. I do wish there was a little more slouch in the calves above the bands, but purely for aesthetic reasons. They’re also really, really soft. Thin but the kind of soft that’s inherently warm. I’ve ordered two more!

COMFORT TO THE MAX MY DUDES Just wanted to give a little point of reference for dimensions, I'm 5'8" and weigh around 135-140 lbs (likely closer to the 140 side as it's comfort food season and I have little to no shame 😎👉👉). From reading the reviews I went up from my usual medium to a large for a little more of the baggy look. So this is SUPER COZY, but I will warn the material is pretty thin and doesn't stand much of a chance in the ugly bitterness of below freezing cold, BUT I feel like there's enough space in there to wear another set of leggings if you really wanted to. LOVE the print and there's a million options to choose from, definitely ordering more especially considering the price. May try to go with my regular size just because the bagginess does gang up around the crotchal area a little, which can be fun but not always the mood I'm looking for. Nevertheless 10/10 would buy these baggy knickers again 👌

Unique shape and seams, trust size chart Update - I did try to wear these for a day, and yes, they were falling off me; definitely recommend ordering typical U.S. size, do not size up like I did! Original review: I am 5'8" tall, U.S. women's size 6, or 28 jeans. According to size chart I should have ordered size 6, but ordered size 8 because I previously got jeans from this store that used the same size chart, but the pants were smaller than stated, and the 8 fit. However, it seems they made these jeans in accordance w/ the size chart this time. Hard to tell from my photos, but they aren't stretching to fit, especially loose in waist, and start sagging as soon as I move around. I'd be constantly pulling them up, so I cannot wear them. It's a denim that stretches more as they are worn too. Go ahead and trust the chart; most likely your U.S. size is the way to go (I often order 1 size up on Amazon/ global sellers). I cannot exchange because I ordered on Vine, but giving 5 stars because I think I'd like them in my proper size. They are both a flare, cropped, and a bit wide-leg, w/ an interesting seam detail that goes down the front. I like the denim, not too heavy w/ a little stretch. Jeans pictured online show 2 different-looking sets- in person, these look most like the looser fitting ones shown, in both color and shape. They are not a typical cut and probably not everyone's cup of tea, but I like things out of the ordinary.

Warm leggings! (Picture on the right) Wore these to an outdoor concert in Colorado and they kept me warm! Temp was 55 degrees with sustained winds between 15-18 mph. Super soft, great fit, highly recommend for fleece lined leggings. I got a medium and I’m 5’1” and 138 lbs. Fit great after a couple times through the washer and dryer. Because of my short height, the crotch was a tad long, but the washing and drying fixed that. The one pocket leggings are definitely more for going out. The three pocket ones have more of an athletic vibe.

Nice warm leggings I was looking for a good pair of fleece lined leggings to keep me warm during the New England winter. I almost always wear a size small in leggings, but after reading other reviews, sized up to a medium in these, which was a great choice as the mediums fit perfect! The picture shows the length - I am almost 5'5 and they bunch a little at my ankle or I can pull them slightly down onto my heel. I really wanted leggings that I could pull over my heel, but these are a little too short to pull all the way over. Over the heel lined leggings are unfortunately, hard to find, which seems odd since you want to cover more skin in the cold! On a side note, I do also have the BALEAF over the heel leggings (not lined) and I wear a size small in those. The lining is nice and definitely warmer than my unlined leggings, and I like that the pockets are also lined. But I would not say these are extremely warm, and often find myself wearing leg warmers over these. I was going back and forth between a 4 and a 5, I would say 4.5!

I got the water resistant and regular. I will say that the sizing of Baleaf leggings is inconsistent. I am 5'7" and 125. I usually wear a small but ordered mediums because I feel like they retain more heat when they aren't skin tight. I got regular fleece lined in the Army Green and water resistant in Cocoa Creme.Regular fleece - the fabric is 75% polyester and 25% spandexWater resistant - the fabric is 77.3 polyester and 22.7% spandex.Water resistant has a tie inside the waist and the regular has no tie. The water resistant also has an additional small pocket inside of the left pocket. The water resistant is larger in general overall but the leg length is about the same on both pair.I held both of them under the tap for a three-count and, much to my surprise, the water resistant pair was, indeed, water resistant. The regular fleece absorbed the water immediately. If left to sit for a minute, the water resistant pair did absorb the water, the same as the regular fleece. The crotch of the water resistant is a single seam. The crotch of the regular fleece is gusseted. Small pocket for a key is in the back on the water resistant; front right of the regular.In my opinion, the regular fleece feels and looks of higher quality but if you need water resistance (but not waterproof, which it's not) I'd get the water resistant pair. Also, I wish these came in different lengths. They are about an inch too short for me.

A bit short but so soft I wish they were two or three inches longer. I usually buy tall Athleta leggings and pay the big bucks for length but I would buy these again because they are so cozy and warm—except for my ankles. I got a small and I’m 5’9” 125 pounds.

Super warm and booty flattering! I have these in both the navy and the black. Purchased xs in both, I'm 115 lbs 5'5" for reference. Fit true to size. I've worn them running, biking, hiking, lounging, and running errands. Sooooo soft and cozy on the inside, pockets on either leg large enough to fit my Samsung Note (good sized phone), plus a small pocket on the waist band for card, key, etc. They been washed several times, turned inside out, and hung to dry with no noticeable wear. Hold their shape well. And, are more flattering than my more expensive cold weather leggings that cost 3 x what these did! A definite staple in my northeastern winter wardrobe! I plan to purchase more colors. Oh, and they're high-waisted, sit right at the smallest part of your waist to nip it right in! Perfect!

Everything is perfect but the crotch area I’m gonna be vulnerable and real honest here. I was so sad these didn’t work out. It hugged my curves so nicely EXCEPT my coochie area 😭 it’s so loose and baggy there yet it makes my fupa pop out way too much. It might be ok for someone skinner but for a woman that’s 5’6 and 185 pounds, it just doesn’t work.

I'm Very Disappointed in the Pocket Version I have owned the fleece lined legging version of this pant for 3 years and loved them but it irks me to walk in the cold with no pocket for my phone. Shoving it in the front of my pants always cause my shirt to get tangled in it and I looked like a crazy person trying to keep my pace and wrestle it in the front of the pants. Enter the pocketed version. I ordered exact same size. Fit like a glove, right under my belly button, though the pocket is tapered and my phone is tight, I didn't think much of it and went on my walk this morning. I only did 1.75mi because I was so disgusted with how far these pants had fallen while walking that I turned around. I attached pics of before and after walk. I pulled them up once while walking and they resumed hip position again almost immediately. Also, due to the tapered pocket, only the top portion of your phone fits in the top, so you have to push, wiggle, wiggle, push, wiggle wiggle to get it far enough in, so it won't fall out...all while trying to keep pace. Within half a mile, I only push, wiggle, wiggled once and it fell right out and hit the ground...in the dark. Whomever designed this pocket, had zero thought about functionality while walking or running. Sure, the pocket looks sleek but it's awful. For reference, I have the S21 which is only exactly 3 inches wide. Do not buy these pants if you have an S10 or the giant Iphone because they won't fit. The only plus about these pants is that the fabric is very lint and pet hair resistant. I'm 5'5"/160# and ordered the large. I'm super pissed that I just wasted over $60 for fleece leggings that are pure caca

The 1st pair was great, others are not consistent I ordered 1 pair of these to start and thought they were great! They fit true to size and felt very comfortable and stretchy (almost like a jeggings material/feel)! Although I don't normally wear jeans in the "long" length, I ordered it in these since the regular length was listed as an inch or so shorter than most regular length jeans. I am 5'5". The long length on the first pair seemed great. They were a little long, but I would rather that and make a small rolled cuff at the bottom than too short. Because I liked the first pair so much that I just got, I ordered 2 more. None of them are consistent. They are all made of different blends of material and have a different texture. The sizing and styles are not consistent. Each pair is a different length. The design on the back pockets of each is different. It does show this in the product photos, but I didn't expect the blends/textures and lengths to also be different. While each pair does fit (some longer than others), the fits do vary. The front pockets are also different. Two of the pairs have what I would consider "normal" front pockets for women's jeans. The 3rd pair has pockets less than 2 inches deep that they're basically pointless! I love the 1st pair I got but will likely send the other 2 back. Just don't expect them to be consistent.

These style of jeans remind me very much of a pair that Jenna Jameson was promoting a few years ago. I recently ripped the inner thigh of those and they were my favourite pair. I searched for any similar styles and came across these ones. I’ve never really been sure about the butt lifting side of things - I don’t have much of a butt to lift, but they do fit really nicely and suck me in around the waist. I was initially concerned as they (I think) are coming from the US so I wasn’t sure about the sizing. I went for my usual sizing and they fit like a dream. Until you get to the length of course! I’m only 4ft 11, so if you’re taller than me these will fit length wise like a dream. But once I chopped off the excess and hemmed them, they’re a perfect fit. The colour is really nice as well on these, not too light, not too dark and I love the unusual design on the back pockets. So much so that I am going to order another pair. The back pocket design does stand out a little but doesn’t make it uncomfortable to sit down on. I am undecided about which pair to get but I’m sure I will either review them or update this review once I have decided. Yes the price is normally more than I’d like to pay, but considering how long the Jenna Jameson style jeans lasted, I think these are worth the price. I tend to wear jeans until they fall to bits anyway, so I don’t foresee any inner thigh rips in my near future.

Flexible & chic I love these Stretchy WERFORU Skinny Elastic Waist Belts with Gold Buckles for accenting dresses, long shirts, dress/work outfits and just overall casual wear. Incredible! Consider buying a size up from your typical size! I usually never buy clothes online because I'll never know if they will fit, but I'm so glad I took a chance with these jeans. Amazon was recommending I go with a size small because I typically wear a size 28 (size 6 or 7 in Old Navy), and I'm so glad I went with a medium based on existing reviews and the minimal difference between size small and medium according to the size chart. I'm a runner, just shy of 5'4'', and my pre-pregnancy weight was around 138-139 pounds. I'm now 18 weeks and my weight has only gone up about 4 pounds, but I was desperate to find some maternity jeans as I was getting increasingly uncomfortable from the pressure resting on my lower abdomen, especially when sitting in a car. I normally wear size 28 (size 6 or 7 in some brands like Old Navy). The inseam length of this size medium is perfect. No area on the jeans feels too tight or too loose. I love how high these sit on the hips because it feels like they aren't going to slip down. I expect there may be some minor shrinkage when I wash them so it may feel a little bit tighter post-wash, but they seem stretchy enough to continue feeling comfortable. I think these will also be awesome postpartum jeans. One thing I was concerned about was that the waistband wouldn't be supportive enough, especially as I get closer to the third trimester, but I simply decided to purchase a separate maternity band for extra support and to wear with workout pants or dresses. Overall, this is a great buy and I recommend for any mamas that are feeling uncomfortable!

**Updated **Update: My fiance actually purchased these for me for Christmas and he had ordered me the Medium and truthfully, the medium fits so much better and the legs still aren't baggy like I was worried about in the first place. These definitely fit more true to size than the Levi maternity shorts do! If you're between sizes I would definitely say you're safe sizing up. I'm 5'4" and pre-pregnancy I weighed about 133 and was wearing either size 6 or 8 AE jeans or 27 in Miss Me and chose to try these in size Small because I had ordered Levi maternity shorts for my last pregnancy and found that even a Small in those ran a little big and I'm glad I went with Small on these instead of Medium. I'm currently 22 weeks pregnant and now up to about 147 and I'm afraid Medium would have been too big and I'm not one for wearing super tight jeans or leggings. I will admit the legs are a little bit tighter than I like but they are stretchy and I still feel like I have room to grow. I'm carrying low and the band snaps under my belly but I'm at a point I can no longer button my normal jeans so these work well and I feel like I should be able to wear these throughout most of the rest of my pregnancy. I would definitely recommend these and if you're on the fence between sizes go for the smaller size otherwise you may end up with baggy "skinny" jeans.

Size Up. Sturdier material like regular jeans, slight stretch. Definitely a slim Fit SIZING: Despite being pull-on jeans, I'd say these fit size wise more like regular jeans in slim. I bought two different brands of pull-on stretch jeans, both in size Medium which is my usual size for Leggings, whereas I'm a size 12 in regular jeans. The other brand fits great, this Chasenot pair I couldn't even pull all the way up to fit. To be fair - I'm curvy and these are slim fitting jeans. I would recommend sizing up - definitely size up if between sizes, and even double size up just to be sure, as I don't think thes in size Large would have worked for me either. For reference I'm 5'4" tall, wear size medium in tops, dresses, and leggings, am about 180#, and carry my weight in my hips and backside. MATERIAL: Quality material, much more like regular jeans that most pull-on stretch jeans. Not a great deal of stretch to these - these aren't in the "leggings" category at all, these are actual jeans that just happen to be pull-on.

Good fit and comfortable I'm 120 lbs and 5'7" and typically wear a slim size 8 for a small waist - these jeans fit me well. The leg length is good as I prefer a pant that covers my ankles - these do. The fabric is a medium weight with a lot of give. These jeans seem to recover their shape ok without getting too stretched-out and droopy. Looking at the inside, the sewing is good but not what I would want for tough work. The pockets are sewn to stretch along with the outer fabric, as they should be. The zipper works ok, not real smooth initially but will likely ease-up after some wear. The button/rivet at the top of the zipper is fine but the buttonhole is a bit too tight. These are decent light-duty to mid-duty denim pants that look nice and are comfortable to wear.

Run slightly small, well made, lighter weight demin. I am 5'7", 164lbs, with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size 10 and 12 in jeans so I got these in a size L. They were a bit tight around the waist but fit. I would recommend sizing up. They are a unique look and are a true barrel jean. The construction of them was high quality. The material is more of a lightweight demin and doesn't feel as high quality as other jeans I own. The material does have some stretch to it.After trying them on I am not a huge fan of this style, so I won't be keeping them. However, if you are looking for a pair of barrel jeans this is a nice budget option. They did not shrink after washing, the pockets are a good size, and they were well made. I just wasn't a fan of the lightweight denim or the style.

PRODUCT HAS CHANGED AS OF 2022 I've been purchasing these socks for years. No doubt supply chain disruptions from the pandemic have caused them to change the fiber makeup of these socks and now they are unwearable. The fabric, which used to fit pretty tightly to my size 8.5 feet, now stretches and twists on my foot so that I'm having to adjust them front to back every fifteen minutes, even just wearing them around my house. You can see how bunched and turned around they get in the first photo. The second picture shows you my attempt to take a stroll around our local lake -- these are NEW socks and I had to stop every fifteen minutes to put them back on, it was almost comical, they were literally falling off my feet. These are NOT the same quality products I've been buying for ten+ years and I advise against buying them.

Comfortable, Good Looking and Warm Fleece Lined Jeans for the Winter I’m 5’-8”, weigh 150 pounds and wear size 10 in AE brand jeans. I ordered size 12 in these since my waist is closer to 30 inches and that’s the size which the manufacturer’s chart recommends. The fit is absolutely perfect, and the length is also spot on. I’m beyond thrilled with this pair of jeans, because they fit and look great and I know they’re going to help me get through the cold winter months. They feel a little bit bulky when wearing them, but that’s to be expected since they’re fleece lined and I’m used to wearing thinner jeans. The front pockets are deep and useful (unlike some pairs of jeans that I’ve owned). The waist is high, there’s a zipper and one riveted button at the top and there are also belt loops. The back pockets are plain and completely free of any fancy stitching. The color is great and they’re very comfortable. The amount of flair on the bottoms is just right. These jeans are very well made and are very good quality.

Nice but a few loose threads I bought these in two different sizes, so I am going to have similar reviews. The fabric on these is very nice, with a soft, smooth finish. They have a good amount of stretch -- better than the PU leather type pants. There was no strange smell to the fabric. The XL and XXL both fit my girlfriend well. She is 5'10" and an average sized American woman. The sizing chart on the product page appears to be accurate. These are very shiny and do a good job of giving the leather look, while still being comfortable and breathable. On the XL, there were some loose threads. I was able to cut them without damaging the integrity of the pants. On the XXL, all of the seams and stitching were perfect. My only other issue, other than the loose threads is that the product description appears to have changed since I ordered these. The product page now refers to these as "jeggings". This are not jeggings, they are leather look leggings. You can see this clearly in the pictures, so I don't know why the page changed. I would recommend these.

Silky smooth, and comfy! Wow! These leggings are fantastic! So silky smooth and comfy, I'm in love! Made out of 81%nylon and 19% Elastane. Your tunic top will slide with these pants! I chose the size small, as I am 119 lbs. and size 4. I am also 5'6. As you can see from my photo, they sit at about slightly above my ankle. I make it work usually. I have some sandals that have ankle straps that will work well and look cute with these. Even heels with ankle straps will look fine too. I would buy more if other colors were available! Love the look and fit! Bendable, stretchable, perfect!

Cute and comfy while they last I will say first that these look nothing like leather in person, however, they are still cute. I'm 5'4 and a size 18 in womens and I got these in a XXL. They fit me just how I'd like. They go past my ankles and rise about 2-3 inches above my belly button. I haven't had any issues with rolling or anything like that. They're not super thick or super thin, there is no see-through of my derriere and above all they are pretty comfortable. Now with all that said I absolutely hate the pockets on these! They're shaped weird and they look even more weird when you have the leggings on. Whenever I wear these, I wear a top that covers the pockets. I would opt to get the version with no pockets. The other unfortunate thing about these is that I don't see these holding up for a long time which is crazy considering the price. I would say that when purchasing these don't expect them to be an everyday type of legging. Think of these as something to be pulled out semi-occassionally that can be dressed up for the impromptu date night or hanging with the girls when you want to be comfortable and cute.

Decent faux leather leggings I’m 175 lbs and 5’6” and these XL faux leather leggings fit well. They are tight but comfortable so be careful with what type of underwear you decide to wear with them. Go with your normal size I really love these pants! They fit great and are very comfortable and flattering. The pockets on the back are a nice touch because I love wearing leggings but sometimes wish they looked more like real pants. The back pockets do just that by making them more versatile pants that can be dressed up or down in a lot of styles! I like the shiny black leather-y kind of look, because it just adds a little touch that differs them from other black denim jeans or jeggings I have. The quality seems great so far. I'll update over time if they wear out quicker than expected or something of the sort. The only downside for me personally is that I ordered the wrong size. I normally wear Large for bottoms (5'4" 185lbs). But, I've gained a few pounds recently and wanted a bigger pair for my closet because even some of my large pants are feeling tight lately. I thought I'd go up a size and get an XL in these just to have a pair that fits me good now. It turns out they're a bit bigger than I expected though, because I feel the looseness of them and constantly have to hike them up, whereas I think they would stay up fine if I'd gotten my normal size. So my suggestion is stick to your normal size because you don't want leggings/stretch based pants to be loose!

Long skinny jeans? Yes please! I am 6' tall, and usually wear between a 7 and 9 in juniors sizes. I went ahead and got a 9 to be safe, and the jeans were still a tad snug. But I think they will stretch a bit with use, and they're extremely comfortable! Also it's so nice to find skinny jeans that actually go down to my ankles! I'll definitely be buying a few more pairs in the different colors! EDIT: I first ordered these in Varsity Blue, and decided to order another pair in Camila because I wanted a darker wash pair as well. However, there is almost no color difference between the two. There were some slight differences between the color of the decorative stitching, however overall there isn't really a noticeable difference in the actually jeans. They are both still very comfortable and my new favorite jeans, especially for the price! I just thought the color difference (or lack thereof) was worth mentioning.

Junior low rise, not jeggings (5'9", 34 inseam on boot cut, size 2-4)... These are... odd. The back of the legs is bigger than the front- so it looks like it tapers in. Idk if anyone would notice but it was enough to bug me. These fit like a tall junior LOW RISE imo but probably comfy in the right size. I got a 5 tall because I'm usually a 2-4 but these were tight. More than long enough A+, but lots of cheap sewing errors. Not like jeggings, just like regular jeans are these days. If you have any butt at all, these will not fit. My butt is super small and looks HILARIOUS in these. I'm guessing would likely fit better in a 7. Idk if I want to try again, but I returned these.

Comfortable classic style. This Denim shirt is a classic. It is not fitted, nor is it boxy so it doesn’t look oversized or sloppy. The fabric is soft against your skin and is lightweight, but not see thru. The sleeves are too long for me, but I have short arms and turn up the sleeves on everything. This shirt feels like one you have been wearing for many years. The dye is darker in some areas, but it looks like it is from years of wear. It is well constructed. I ordered size Large, my usual size and according to their size chart. It fits great. I am 5’3” tall, 153 pounds, measure 41-31-41 for reference. Great top.

Great wardrobe basic! This denim shirt from luvamia is the type of wardrobe basic that you will reach for again and again. Layer it under a vest, wear it solo, layer it on top of a tank or tshirt, wear it open like a jacket, you'll never run out of options. This top is lightweight, yet sturdy 100% cotton. I picked this up for my daughter, she is 5'4" and 108lbs. She is wearing the size small in the helium blue. Made in China, this denim top appears to be well made. All button, seams and hems are nicely sewn and have held up well to repeated washings. We machine washed in cold water and line dried. It washed up nice and soft with minimal shrinkage and no color fading or pilling. Overall this is a great looking, easy wear top that will get a lot of milage in your wardrobe!

I am kind of in between size, medium, and size large, and I ordered the classic blue. First of all, the color is darker than the picture shows. They still are not real dark blue, but they are darker than shown. As for the size, I ended up getting the large and they are just a tad loose. I am 5‘4“ and 145 pounds. Also, they are long and come all the way down to my feet. The sanding on the backside is a little strange. Both legs are exactly the same shading under the pockets, but the positioning makes my saddlebags look wider than they are. I may have to get some sandpaper or pumice and try to add some more to the front and the back. I guess when you pay less money, you’re not getting an individualized shading, but all of the pairs will be the same. The rips are at the knees and nicely done. It seems like the fringe on the bottom of the right leg is about a half inch longer than the left leg also. They are very comfortable, and I suggest if you are in between sizes, maybe go with the smaller size. I feel like these are going to be a bit baggy after wearing them for a while.
fun flare I'm 5'2" and these Women High Waisted Cropped Ripped Jeans fit me wonderfully. Comes exactly as described. I will match these with statement sandals or sneakers.

Cute Style These are flare jeans that are high-waisted and cropped. I am 5,2 and these come to me as a regular pant they don't seem cropped for me because of my height. They are a very soft Gene material that has rips here and there. They are high-waisted and fit very well for me. I ordered a size based on the size chart, but I think I could have gone down one size smaller. They are very comfortable because of the material and the fact that it's softer than a standard jean. The raw hem at the bottom makes them look young and cute.

Perfect fit! Picture was taken right out of package, so a little wrinkled. These jeans are very trendy and cute! They fit me perfectly and are a great length for my 5’3” body. I wear a size 12, these stretch a bit so I think if you wear a 14 it shouldn’t be a problem.These washed up perfectly, didn’t have any more fraying either. Very comfortable and just enough stretch to move with your body. These are definitely made for shorter people like me, I would still recommend!

Great pair of stretchy wide legged jeans My wife can be somewhat complicated to buy for as she is only 4'10" and trying to understand measurements when buying clothes online can be hit or miss. Happy to say that these jeans only require a little shortening but otherwise they fit her perfectly. She likes that they have a stretchy fit so you don't feel like you are suffocating in them. The design is very nice and the materials and craftsmanship are very good. We would highly recommend these.

Soft and super comfy stretchy jeans. According to the size chart measurements I decided that a size 16 would be the right one for me. These actually have a lot of stretch in them, so I maybe could have gone for a 14 instead as I do occasionally have to hitch these pants up again, lol. Not the fault of the pants though! They are made of 71% cotton, as stated on the product page and on the tag inside the jeans, so they may shrink when I wash and dry them, and that will help with the slipping down. They have four functioning pockets, two front with flaps that snap closed and two back pockets without flaps. They are somewhat short, so that makes them great for my height (5' 4") and under, I think. Even on me, they just barely get to my ankles. The material is sort of thick, so I think they will be nicely warm in the winter. I have worn them a couple times with temperatures around 70 F and usually I am happy to get home and put something cooler on. I love the color of them and the comfy fit. With all the stretch they have, there is no binding or pinching, no matter what direction you can contort your legs into. They are well made and all around just a nice pair of jeans, especially for shorter people. I definitely can recommend this pair of jeans. Comfort and fit are great with them.

They are somewhat short, so that makes them great for my height (5' 4") and under, I think. Even on me, they just barely get to my ankles. The material is sort of thick, so I think they will be nicely warm in the winter. I have worn them a couple times with temperatures around 70 F and usually I am happy to get home and put something cooler on. I love the color of them and the comfy fit. With all the stretch they have, there is no binding or pinching, no matter what direction you can contort your legs into. They are well made and all around just a nice pair of jeans, especially for shorter people. I definitely can recommend this pair of jeans. Comfort and fit are great with them.

Cute & Comfy, But Lose Around My Waist & Over My Hips Cute and Comfy would definitely define these pants. The pants exhibits exquisite quality providing maximum stretch and ease. Being around children I have to constantly bend down, pick up things and I am running errands. At moments like these, I prefer to be comfortable and want to make a fashion statement at the same time. I like the style of the pants and them being wide leg they look fine over my legs. Where they fall a bit big is by the waistline and the hips. I ordered a size small in white and the length of the pants is perfect since I'm 5'2, but they are not cropped in that sense, and I don't mind them being full length pants, I actually like them being that way. The pants waist does have loops so I can wear a belt fixing the issue of them being loose. I believe for someone who has a waist line of 27' or 28' inches wouldn't need a belt. The bottom opening of the pants is about 9' inches. I like that the pants have nice front pockets where you can easily slide your phone or a thin card wallet. I would recommend these pants because I find them to be comfortable and stylish overall.

smells awful but great length I HATE pants that ride up at the ankles, especially when I sit down. I want mine touching the ground (although preferably not dragging). They’re honestly a smidge big, but they fit my waist without feeling like they’ll fall off, and I don’t like my pants to be too snug anyways. That said, the smell really is terrible. I haven’t made up my mind if I’ll keep them. If they shrink and come out crisp from the wash, it’s worth it, but if they don’t, it’s a waste of money.

Cargo girlie I recently purchased these cargo pants in a size medium. As someone who is 5'5" and weighs 160 pounds, medium is generally my go-to size. I found these pants to fit well without needing to size up or down. The length, while adequate, could have been slightly longer for my preference, but it’s by no means a deal-breaker. The fit is comfortable—not too tight—and there’s a nice bit of stretch, which adds to the overall comfort and ease of movement. These pants are perfect for both casual wear and more active outings. If you’re considering buying these, I’d recommend sticking to your usual size. Overall, I’m happy with my purchase and would definitely recommend them to others looking for versatile and comfortable cargo pants!

Stylish and comfortable high-waist jeans but beware of the sizing and length! I have a lot of good things to say about these jeans! First off, the high waist is just perfect, it cinches the waist beautifully, creating a flattering silhouette. I also love the wide-leg style; it gives a lengthening effect that makes my legs look longer and adds a touch of retro chic to my look. The square pockets are a unique and also have a bit of a vintage vibe, which I adore. The classic denim is another highlight. It’s thick and stretchy, very comfortable,great for cooler autumn and winter weather. Plus, they’re super easy to style. I find they pair especially well with crop tops, creating a trendy, well-put-together outfit. Despite the quality and style, though, I’m not entirely satisfied with the Fit. I always order a Medium, but these jeans run large. I need a belt to keep them up. Also, the length is a bit much for me, given that all sizes have the same 30" inseam. At 5’2” and 116 pounds, they’re too long for my frame. If you’re on the shorter side and don’t want to hem them, keep in mind that they might be a bit lengthy. Overall, for style and quality, these jeans deserve a solid 5/5. If I were taller, the small size would have fit perfectly. Just be mindful of the sizing if you’re petite!

Comfortable and stylish jeans! I am very pleasantly surprised by these jeans. They are extremely comfortable and they look great too! I think jeans can be tricky to get right. They’re either too tight or too baggy or they’re too long or too short but these are “Goldilocks jeans” because they are just right 👏. The material is soft and stretchy so they have some give in them. I ordered a small and they fit perfectly around the hips and waist without feeling tight. The product description said the inseam was 30” and I knew this was the exact length I needed when wearing boots with a heel. I’m 5’4” but I do have short legs so although these work great for me, they may be too short for many people of the same height. If I had any criticism, it would be that the stitching wasn’t great in some areas but I’ve been able to tidy it up using a pair of small nail scissors. Overall, I couldn’t be happier with how these jeans look and feel! And, you certainly can’t beat the price point because jeans can be expensive. I highly recommend 😊.

wash first - but recommend! Unfortunately I didn't take a picture of the packaging, however, they come rolled in a plastic bag. The problem is that they were "moist" and therefore had a smell which is why I removed a star.For reference, I am 5'3, wear a very loose size 12, tight size 10 (US sizes). I ordered a medium which according to the size chart, this should be for US sizes 8-10. I ordered a Medium as an incentive to push me to lose weight/fit into these pants by the end of the year. As you can see by the picture, they fit now - love it!! They are NOT tight, they are loose, comfortable and can bend with ease. They are VERY stretchy and while I don't necessarily like how they look on me yet, BUT you can see that they are very well made.The stitching is great, the pockets are very deep. I put my iPhone16 (for reference) in the front pocket and bent forward and it didn't come out. I place it in the back pocket and it moved up a bit but 90% of my phone was still in the pocket so it did not fall out which is always a plus! The pants are a bit long but I am short (again, I am 5'3) so short heels/boots will fix the issue. The pockets don't "bulge open" because of the material and they are really soft and comfortable!Highly recommend but wash these first - no, they didn't shrink.

Excellent quality and craftsmanship I knew that there was no risk in ordering Aeropostale clothes without trying them on first. For reference, I am 5 feet 5 inches tall and weigh 130 pounds. I usually wear medium-sized clothing, and I got these pants in medium size, which is US sizes 6-8 according to the measurement chart provided by the seller. The pants are a bit loose on me. They are more of a size 8 than 6. My medium is more of a size 6. I do not know if they will shrink a little after a wash. I also got a regular inseam, which is 32 inches, which is too long for me. I will have to make them shorter by at least an inch. Other than that, the pants fit my body nicely. There is nothing weird about their cut or shape. I like the fabric. It is very soft and has a little bit of stretch to it. The pants are very comfortable. The craftsmanship is impeccable. So I would highly recommend these pants if you were looking for a pair of casual ones.

Comfy; stretchy I’m 5’3”, 135lbs., normally wear an 27/28 or 6/7. I carry my weight in my bum/thigh/lower belly. I ordered a size small. Wasn’t expecting to like these as much as I do! They run a little on the big side, but in a good way. They are a loose fit and have a nice stretch, but they aren’t too loose and baggy. I would say order the size you normally wear; no need to size up. They’re totally comfy due to the stretch. They are def high-waisted. I don’t always look that flattering in high-waisted pants, but I feel good in these! My legs are short, so these look more like high waters on me rather than a capris, but I like the look with boots. But if you have short legs and want them shorter, keep in mind you may need to hem them. Planning to hang dry since they are quite stretchy and I don’t want to damage the elastic. They aren’t the best/thickest quality, which means they likely won’t last too long, but I bet they’ll be comfy when the weather starts to warm up. I was quite happy that I ordered these. We will see how they hold up!
