Very stretchy and fit like a second skin. My thighs are the thickest part of my body. I need bottoms that accommodate my thighs first and usually always have to get the waist taken in. These would likely stretch out as I wore them and get baggy in the waist. I might consider a small over a medium, but the chance it would not fit my thighs becomes the issue. I’ll never know. The wash is awful! I’m returning them specifically for that reason. They are very dark blue, and the “worn” or bleached spots are super contrasted. It doesn’t match the image from the listing. It’s giving early jegging vibes. Pass. Maybe a different color would be worth it. Overall, I would recommend for anyone who’s curvy, with a hip to waist ratio of more than 9-10inches. The material isn’t see through and you might get a summer out of them before the material wears through in the thighs.

Not like photo or video I'd give these a 3.5 stars. I should have noted that the photos used for the black pair do not show the backside and are strange looking pictures. The pair I got looks like you sat in something like ashes...it's washed out gray and weird looking on the parts you sit on. Can I live with it? Not sure yet. I ordered two pairs and got these first. The second pair I ordered in army green and are two weeks away. I will make a decision then. In the meantime, I will dwell on whether the distressed washed out greyish backside is too strange. I can't stop seeing it...my eyes just go there. Like a lot of black jeans, they have that weird chemical smell. Will it go away? Maybe? Probably? Not sure yet. Next thing: the video shows a black pair with hardly any distressing...which is not what I got. I read tons of reviews and decided to get my usual size 4. Because they are wide legged...not a problem fit wise anywhere. And I have read so many reviews these were long. I am 5'5" with long legs and these hit me a couple inches above my ankle. I would not call these capris. I would call them cropped, which is what I wanted. These were a little snug in the waist, and again...nothing like what was presented in the video which made them appear to be really stretchy. There's some. The other rather unexpected thing is the big wide, bell flare. They don't look that wide in any pictures. So that was the critical portion. Here's the positive: They are well made. Have some stretch to them, are on trend for today and a reasonable dupe of the Billabong Free Fall for about half the price. The front pockets are real functioning pockets and the back pockets are in the right places. These can be dolled up or down depending on your mood. Still on the fence about being ok looking like you sat in something and the super wide bell bottom.

Perfect vintage slim (not skinny) ankle jean Absolutely love these! They call them a skinny jean, but they are not what I think of as skinny. They are a slim fit. They also are not full length and more of an ankle jean. For reference, I am 5’3”, 118 lbs. I purchased based on the photo rather than how the jeans were described and they fit exactly as I hoped. The vintage distressing is also perfect. I like the vintage look but I’m 51 and don’t want gaping holes or tears in my jeans. These are perfect, comfortable, soft, and no waist gap. I rated 4 stars only because Democracy sews tags into the waistband that are so irritating to my skin, and I have to use my seam ripper to remove them. It’s annoying, but finding jeans that I love is so hard this is just a minor inconvenience.

I'm going to order another pair down a size and see how they look less looser than what I ordered because looking at the size chart according to the waist measurement I should have ordered a size down but I'm happy with the jeans I ordered because I wanted them bigger/loose anyways, more like actual boyfriend jeans fit which is exactly what I got. Update edit 10/20/23, I brought the size 1 a size down and the fit is even better, still loose in the legs and comfortable around the waist not tight but also not overly loose as the size 3 jeans. I still like the bigger one's though because I like having all different fits and styles in jeans but going down a size is more flattering to my shape yet still loose and boyfriend jeans styled still not a skinny jeans look. Also fyi I ordered the medium color denim but received the light color denim instead, at first I debated on returning because I received the wrong color but ultimately I liked them so much that I decided to keep them. But I read other review complaints about receiving the wrong color as well so just beware that you may or may not receive the right color of blue ordered.

Thank you reviews that recommended sizing down First off, I LOVE these jeans! Secondly, the reviews I read on here were a huge help in sizing. It’s weird because this are definitely Women’s jeans, but the sizing is in Juniors. For reference I am 6’0, 170-175 lbs, and a solid Women’s size 10/30, which would translate to a Juniors size 11. Per the suggestion of previous reviews, I reluctantly sized down to a 9. The last thing I wanted was for the jeans to fit for an hour, only to stretch beyond repair. I ordered a size 9 with 34” inseam. I am SO happy I did! When I pulled it out the bag I thought to myself, there’s NO WAY this is gonna work. But I squeezed myself into the initially doubtful fit. Wisely I decided to wear them around the house for a couple hours, w/o removing tags (just in case.. again, because of reviews). And to my pleasant surprise this wonderful pair of jeans decided to form to my figure in the best ways possible. So if you’re in doubt and you got some junk in the trunk, size down and trust the process. Because I could totally see if I had bought these in an 11, I wouldn’t like them now. Also the 34” inseam does almost hit the floor for me, but the next pair I order will be a 36”, just so I have some extra length.

Buyer Beware: Poor Fabric Quality in Samantha I purchased these jeans in 3 colors: Dark Blue (8/13/21), Quinn (2/15/22), and Samantha (4/24/22). All 3 were in size 5 and fit great (I'm about 5'5" 120 pounds). Dark Blue has a smoother fabric that’s not quite denim and while the leg cut is the same style, the back pockets are different, they’re more trouser looking. Quinn and Samantha are the same style, both have the “W” logo stitching in the back pockets. Quinn was made of thicker/sturdier denim and cost $50 but Samantha cost $69 and is in a thinner cheaper denim. Samantha being the newest one barely lasted 3 months because there's a rip along the back pocket. Thankfully I noticed the tear while folding laundry and was not out in public. Each pair isn’t cheap but for Samantha to cost the most at $69.00, I would expect it to last way more than 3 months!

90's cowgirl dream jeans! (the "brand size" column on the sizing chart is key to finding your fit) The reviews on the sizing of these jeans helped me tremendously, so I figured I'd do my part and pass along some information. I've had my eyes on these jeans for about a year, and when I saw that the "antique wash" version was on sale for only $20, I knew it was time to pull the trigger and a new denim staple to my fall wardrobe. My only question was...what size? When I looked at the reviews, it was clear that I needed to study the sizing charts before making my decision. My measurements: height: 5' 7" waist: 26.5 in. hips: 38 in. thigh: a little less than 24 in. inseam: 32 in. On the Wrangler's website, the sizes on the sizing chart are all listed in even numbers while the sizes offered here on Amazon are in odd numbers, so that makes things confusing. On the Wrangler's website, my waist lines up with a size 0, my hips with a size 6 and my thighs with a size 14. According to Amazon, my waist lines up with a 0, my hips with a size 5 and my thighs with an 11. On the Amazon sizing chart, the very first column says "brand size". As far as I can tell, the first number represents the waist measurement and the second number represents the in-seam. These numbers are really and may be the most in determining what size to try. I knew the jeans were supposed to have kind of a baggy fit around the thighs, but I wanted them to be more fitted across the hips and backside area so they still gave off a feminine vibe. I decided to try size 5-32 first. The length was perfect. They fit well over my cowboy boots, rainboots and lace-up/hiking boots. However, the waist was gaping and the booty and thigh area were way too loose for my taste. I decided to send them back and order a 3-32 in their place. I am much, much happier with the 3-32's. The material is stiff and feels like traditional, high quality denim, but they are not too tight anywhere. They feel equally feminine and tom-boyish (which is my style, in a nutshell). I could wear these to do yard/barn work or I could dress them up a little with a girly top and different shoes and still feel cute. The $20 markdown was too good to pass up, and I'm really glad I took the time to find my size. On the Amazon size chart under "brand size", the 3-32's are listed as 27-32. Again, I have a 26.5in waist and a 32in inseam (basically 27-32), and that is the size that ended up working for me. The rest of the waist, hip and thigh measurements that correspond to the 27-32 row on the Amazon chart do not reflect my measurements at all, but had I based my purchase solely off the "brand size" column here on Amazon, I would have gotten the sizing right the first time. I hope this review helps somebody out there who's looking for a great pair of jeans. :) Sizes purchased: 3-32 and 5-32 (compared in the photos as "3" and "5", respectively) Size kept: 3-32

According to Amazon, my waist lines up with a 0, my hips with a size 5 and my thighs with an 11. On the Amazon sizing chart, the very first column says "brand size". As far as I can tell, the first number represents the waist measurement and the second number represents the in-seam. These numbers are really and may be the most in determining what size to try. I knew the jeans were supposed to have kind of a baggy fit around the thighs, but I wanted them to be more fitted across the hips and backside area so they still gave off a feminine vibe. I decided to try size 5-32 first. The length was perfect. They fit well over my cowboy boots, rainboots and lace-up/hiking boots. However, the waist was gaping and the booty and thigh area were way too loose for my taste. I decided to send them back and order a 3-32 in their place. I am much, much happier with the 3-32's. The material is stiff and feels like traditional, high quality denim, but they are not too tight anywhere. They feel equally feminine and tom-boyish (which is my style, in a nutshell). I could wear these to do yard/barn work or I could dress them up a little with a girly top and different shoes and still feel cute. The $20 markdown was too good to pass up, and I'm really glad I took the time to find my size. On the Amazon size chart under "brand size", the 3-32's are listed as 27-32. Again, I have a 26.5in waist and a 32in inseam (basically 27-32), and that is the size that ended up working for me. The rest of the waist, hip and thigh measurements that correspond to the 27-32 row on the Amazon chart do not reflect my measurements at all, but had I based my purchase solely off the "brand size" column here on Amazon, I would have gotten the sizing right the first time. I hope this review helps somebody out there who's looking for a great pair of jeans. :) Sizes purchased: 3-32 and 5-32 (compared in the photos as "3" and "5", respectively) Size kept: 3-32

Heavyweight jeans like they used to make I'm 5'3", 152 lbs, 32" waist, 39" hips. Based on reviews and the size chart’s waist measurement, I ordered size 13; otherwise, I would have ordered an 11. The waist was comfortable with a little room to spare. However, the hips and thighs were too roomy for my taste. I would say these would fit a true hourglass shape (with ample hips and thighs) very nicely. If I were to do it again, I would've gone with the 11. Of the six I ordered under Prime Try Before You Buy, these are made of the heaviest weight denim (98% cotton, 2% spandex) and are the most reminiscent of jeans you could buy in the 80s. I suspect they would hold up the best as work jeans. I do like them, and might try again in a smaller size in the future, but due to the fit and having six others to choose from, I’m sending these back. Good quality but sizing inconsistent Ordered both a Large ( my recommended size per Amazon) and an XL per reviewers saying it was tight in the chest (I’m a 38B). The XL came first and fit fine with a shirt underneath and comfortable. Then the Large came and it also fit comfortably so I decided to measure them and found they were identical in size 🤷🏼‍♀️. Both were 21” across at chest (42”) and 33 ¾” in length from top of shoulder. I’m 5’4 1/2” and it is above the knee so a great length for me. Both are great quality denim fabric, well made and flattering on as well as comfortable. Both tags say 85% cotton 15% polyester ( not spandex). As to stretch, there is stretch up and down but Not side to side as one might expect. I decided to keep both as it’s such a great versatile wardrobe piece that is hard to find. Just don’t know if this is a fluke that both sizes were identical or what. 🤷🏼‍♀️

The best throwback dress of the season! I am mildly obsessed with this dress and absolutely cannot wait for it to be warm enough to wear! It is just about the cutest style ever, made even better by the lightweight but traditional, no stretch acid wash denim. This material means it holds its shape without clinging. The style is extremely flattering, it’s like it’s made for beer belly days;). The top and sleeves are cut in a way to still show a bit of shape so you aren’t totally shapeless.The material is not soft but I think if I put it in the dryer instead of hand dry, it’ll help that. I took off a star because there is a very big missed opportunity for pockets.The buttons are functional thankfully, because I do have to unbutton it to take off (since there is no stretch).I am 5’6, 160 lbs, 34D, and tend to carry a tad put off weight in mid section. I am a true medium and so is this dress, so very true to size. Nice length for my height and preference.

Easy to wear and flattering I like the fit and flattering style of this LookbookStore Women's Short Sleeve Button Down Flowy Tiered Babydoll Denim Dress. For size reference, I am 5'4", pear shaped, 36b, size 10, 150lbs. I picked up the medium in the reef blue and I am happy with the color and the fit. It is a good length on me. My calves aren't bad, but above the knee things take a turn for the worse. This dress hits me just right, short enough to be cool and comfortable in the summer, but long enough to fully hide my thighs. The empire waist is flattering and gives me shape without overwhelming me with ruffles. It is an interesting design, as the tiers are only in the back. The front is flat with just the one seam at the waist. The sleeve is nice as well, decent in length to hide my upper arms. Made in China from 85% cotton, 15% polyester, this dress appears to be well made, the buttons are well sewn and all the seams and hems are finished nicely. Despite the cotton/polyester blend, the fabric is not stretchy at all, but I went with my usual size and it fits well. I just wanted to point out that I am not at all busty, I carry my weight in the bottom, so this style dress works well for me. If you have a large chest or are very broad across your back, you may find this dress too restricting. Overall, I am very happy with this dress and think it will serve me well this summer, even into the fall. This can be worn very casually with a pair of Converse sneakers to a back yard picnic, or dressed up a bit with a sexy strappy sandal and a great chunky bracelet for dinner out. It would even work well as a bathing suit cover up. I could also see this with boots and a little cardigan for fall. It washed up well, I did a cold water wash and hung it to dry. There was minimal shrinkage and no color fading, it didn't wrinkle too badly either. Bottom line, a great wardrobe basic that you will reach for often!

Poorly constructed ; way too expensive for this many fabrication defects Positives : Fit is good on my 5'5", 135lbs frame. Rise is the height I prefer. Negatives : Pocket has hole from poor construction and bottom hems were sewn incorrectly - stretched out - so they don't lay right and look sloppy, fabric is kinda thick clingy stretchy, and pockets are TINY I would've been angry if I had paid full price for these. This is my 1st experience with the Lucky brand, and, while the fit is what I want, I probably wouldn't buy Lucky jeans again following this experience because of the low quality construction.

What you ORDER is NOT what you GET! I ordered the 31w x 29L what I received is 12/31A! How is a 31 length considered ankle? It is also not 29 inches in length as advertised. These pants happen to fit me around the waist but they are very long. I'm 5'1 I specifically buy my jeans short. I'm not sure if a size 12 is equal to a 31w. Each brand seems to vary. I don't know that these are real Lucky brand Jeans they are soft and comfortable Jeans and if you are ok with the length and size then I would say get them. Especially if they are on sale for $29.00 you can always return them if they don't work out.

Shockingly well-fitting and comfortable I was not expecting such a great fit -- perfect, really -- of these on me. I'm 5'5", about 142lbs, typically size 4-6 in most major brands, and the size 6 fits perfectly. No waist gap, no uncomfortable snugness anywhere, hits right at my waist; even the length works for me. The wide legs are pretty wide, bordering on baggy; I think I'm going to get them tailored slightly so they're less palazzo-like and drape more similarly to Levi's Ribcage Wide Leg (which I love the width of the legs, but Levi's just generally do not fit me well in the waist, butt, and thigh areas). These have a bit of stretch as well. I love them. I got them in another color, and have another on the way -- they're that great!

Great jeans but too long These jeans are very comfortable, high waisted but not too high(just right). They are soft and stretchy . They are very fashionable . Cypress color not as expected & be aware of rhinestones This is the first time I have ordered a pair of Wallflower jeans. I ordered the Cypress shade in particular because I wanted light wash jeans with a pale blue color. As you can see from the photos, these jeans are definitely quite a bit darker than the photos of Cypress jeans on the model. The next thing I was surprised about is probably my own fault because I just didn't notice them in the online images. Be prepared if you order the Cypress jeans to have slightly bedazzled jeans. There are small rhinestones across the back pockets. There are also 2 larger rhinestones, one on either side at the corner of the front pockets. I'm not a big fan of that, but the huge plus for me with these jeans was the perfect fit. I'm almost 5'7" and 130 pounds with wider hips and the size 7 Regular fits me to perfection. (Note: I plan to wear these with boots/shoes with about 2 inch heels. The length would be slightly too long for me if in flats.) So because I love the fit so much and they seem like high quality jeans, the darker denim color and the rhinestones are not a deal breaker. Just wanted other customers aware.

Nice Pants! I'm 5'08", 146. I bought a size 9 Long. The pants are the correct length and won't drag. I wear a 31" inseam. The pants aren't very stretchy, and they are a tad bit tight in the waist. I should have sized up one size. However, I'll make them work. The buttons and zipper all function. The stitching is good. The material isn't very thick, but I like that. They material smelled fine. I would buy this product again.

Inconsistent sizing Bought my 1st pair in April 2024. I liked them so much that I bought 3 more. All jeans purchased were size 13 reg. The 3 I just received were at least 3-4 inches longer. Why the change? As much as I wish I was 5'8" tall. I'm only 5'4". Disappointed in the sizing change.

Comfortable, Reg length is long I’m 61, 5’3”, 179 lbs - Age is important, because it changes body shape. I’m excited to find these jeans, so I bought 4 different pairs, hoping something would work. Three of 4 are keepers. I particularly like the stretch, because they’re as comfortable as yoga pants. I don’t mind the waist being a little loose, and it has a slight gap. I’d rather it didn’t cut in and make a roll. It’s not to the point I’m pulling them up. I have thick thighs and don’t want pull lines you get when you cross from fitted to too small jeans. I’m at that age that comfort is important, but so is style. 😁 Over 10 years ago a blogger, Grasping For Objectivity, did a big series on jeans, showing how important pocket placement is along with pictures of the same women in different jeans. It was the only article like that, and it’s still the best source as it showed comparisons of real women and told the brands. (Never buy ON) Why is this relevant? THESE JEANS have the RIGHT pocket placement and aren’t remotely close to $100. What. A. Score! These will be my go-to from now on. I found the inseam on regular is longer than many other brands. At 5’3” I should always need petite length but have a short waist and long legs, so I usually require regular length, especially in cheap brands like Lee. You can see that regular length is very long on me. I could’ve ordered petite, but I don’t mind. If you wear heals, this would be perfect. I will buy these again. I’ve found my go-to jeans!

Best jeans for women with curves I love this brand of jeans. I’ve been wearing them for years. I was so happy to see that Amazon has them now. The luscious fit is the absolute best for anyone who has curves or an hourglass shape. It has extra room in the hips and butt while still keeping the waistband snug. I notoriously have issues with pants when they fit my waist properly. They are so tight on my thighs and butt. But these jeans are great. I also like that they have a very slight boot cut. I ordered my true size which is a size one and they fit well. For reference, I am 5 foot five and 116lbs measurements 26 inch waist and 38 inch hips. No complaints.

Comfy but a little baggy These are very comfy jeans. The length was good. I'm 5'2" so sometimes even the short length is too long for me. These don't pool at my feet but give me enough that I can wear a boot or platform. I'm not crazy about the pocket placement. They are wide and low. The bottom of the pocket sits below the bottom of my cheek. Like under the booty ledge. It makes my butt look weird. I have to wear them with a shirt that covers it. For the price I think they are good enough.

STRETCH comfort I’ve been working on losing weight this year, and had reached a point where I was forced to purchase some jeans. I had been wearing my 16s with a belt, and then my 14s also with a belt, as I dropped lbs (luckily I’d kept jeans I’d outgrown!) but luckily for me, they are straight leg and the baggy/loose fit is in fashion - they fit and looked like denim trousers. I’ve been waiting to purchase new jeans until my goal weight is determined (IF lifestyle has changed my world!). Since I may un-grow these at some point again I didn’t want to spend a lot. Black Friday sales had these at $15. I couldn’t beat that so I got 2 pair- one I thought would fit and 1 as a goal pair. This is the “I thought they might fit” pair…size 11L. I’m 152lbs, 33” inseam. I LOVE THEM. The cut and fit is PERFECT for me.

Finally a good jean I have been trying to find a nice, comfortable pair of jeans that weren't skinny or above my belly button. I always have an issue with pants buttons pushing into my gut any time I sit down. This is why I avoid wearing anything but relaxed leggings, which is anything but classy. At 5'4" and 175lbs, I have an even hourglass shape, I'm usually a size 16. So I went with the 16 short and liked them for the most part. The issue was that they were a little baggy in the crotch and had a habit of sliding down. I sent them back and got a 15 short and they are perfect and the length is nice, I don't feel like I'm gonna trip over the hems. I can't even tell that I'm wearing jeans, they fit as comfortably as my leggings. This actually caused me to forget to undo them when I took them off. I included a squatting picture just to emphasize how stretchy and comfortable they are. The pictures with the green shirt are size 16 short, the purple shirts are the size 15 short. I would one hundred percent recommend these pants. I intend to get another pair later.

New favorite jeans! I have a hard time finding jeans that fit! I'm 5'0" tall and about 105 lbs. I bought size 1 Short. Fantastic for athletic figures I only ordered these because I was so intrigued by the amount of positive reviews. I studied all the comments to get the sizing correct and that did not work! I am 5’9” that is a true size 6 BUT I have muscular quads, and glutes from weightlifting and my waist is normal. I ordered a size 11 tall. Way too big. Landed with 5 Tall and I use a belt. If you have a small waist/big thigh & glutes a 3 would work. For an athletic build these are SO COMFORTABLE and fit great over boots. A true boot leg. If you want to wear with flat sneakers maybe don’t go Tall.

Go to Jeans, love, stitching lasted 2 months :( 5'8" 125 lbs. Waist 26" hips 37" Usually wear a 5 or 7 long in jeans. Ordered 7 Long. I love these jeans. I have the hardest time finding jeans. They hug my form, sit under my belly button, they are long enough for my 33" inseam, they are beyond comfortable. fell.in.love. I ordered them 12/20 and they came out of the wash today 2/23 - 2 months later- with a 1 1/2" unraveled seam in the waistband. So bummed. I will stitch them up, but 2 months is too short of a lifespan for jeans. These are my going out jeans, so I have maybe washed regular cycle and then tumbled dry low a total of 5 or 6 times. I want to 5 star them so badly, but I expected them to last longer. I'm old fashioned and don't want rips in my jeans ; )

Love these! I got the Jenna color. I thought they would be a tad darker but it’s not off enough to return. I’m 5’3 around 165-170ish unsure right now. I normally wear a 14 in stretchy denim or a 16. I got the 15 short in these jeans and they fit perfectly! I love the rise which are below my belly button I hate high rise. These have some stretch to them and is a nice soft denim. I’m totally shocked they fit! I normally try on 25 pairs of jeans to find one pair that fits. I bought two different pairs two different brands totally expecting to return one of them if not both. Right now if other pair fits I’m keeping both because these are keepers! The other pair will have to be perfect because they are $80. This is a great Jean at a good price.

Fit like a glove right out of the package! As most people who shop for various products online , I was a bit hesitant to buy jeans sight unseen. Some things you just HAVE to try on. But for 30 bucks and a simple return policy, I figured I’d give it a shot. I had zero pairs of jeans that fit well and I was getting ready for a mini vacation. GOOD CALL! These babies for great right out of the box! The rise is perfect (about an inch below bellybutton) and they aren’t too snug in the hips or thigh area. (Gotta love stretch denim👍🏻) But most importantly, the length/inseam was spot-on for what I like. I am almost 6 feet tall and around 170lbs and can fit anywhere between a 9 and 13, depending on the cut and brand. They look great with boots, tennis shoes, AND flats! I have the hardest time finding a jean that looks good on my frame without trying to dress like a teenager and I try to avoid the horrid “mom jeans” at all costs. (I’m 48). After a washing they still fit great. They get just a bit loose around the waist at the end of the day (as most jeans do) but a decent belt solves that problem easily. I will definitely be snatching up the other cuts and colors from Wallflower! I have had a compliment from every person I’ve seen that knows me in the last few days, even one person that asked me if I had lost weight. Nope! Just a SUPER cute, awesome fitting pair of jeans! I feel like my wardrobe has expanded now that I can wear shirts that I haven’t been wearing because I feel uncomfortable showing off the ol’ muffin top. Problem solved! Thanks Wallflower for catering to the real woman, and not just the teenie bopper stick figure... definitely one extra satisfied customer here!

Ordered 29inseam, got 31, even though tag said 29? Grrrr Ordered short, received mismarked 31in inseam. Jeans are adorable and fit great except for length. So they sent wrong size and they must have issues with production line. But flippin cute so 2 stars and I might try again...

Best jeans I've ever purchased! You need these! I am 5'6" 178 lbs and curvy. My waist is small but my hips are large. I have a hard time finding jeans that fit my hips and legs but aren't too big in the waist. These fit me perfectly! They are stretchy, soft, and feel like they were made for my body. I'm shaped like a real woman. I usually wear a size 14 so I ordered a 15 and 13 in these jeans. The 15 fits great but after wearing them a few hours tend to stretch out and become a little loose around my waist. The 13s fit a tiny bit snug around the waist when first putting them on, but within a few hours stretch into the perfect fit. I was skeptical about buying jeans online but figured since I could return them for free, I'd take a chance. So glad I did!! I ordered 4 more pairs last night! I will try to add pics later. Also the colors and washes of the jeans are true as advertised. I will never buy another brand of jeans again. The material is great quality and I feel as though these jeans will last a long time. I am used to wearing scrubs a LOT so if I say an article of clothing is comfortable you can guarantee they are extraordinarily comfy!

My favorite jeans I love these jeans. I normally wear a size 4 or 27 women's. I think these are juniors sizes. The 3 fits perfectly. NOT ACTUALLY "CURVY" FIT I'm looking for a soft pair of jeans that don't constrict (I haven't yet been able to embrace the leggings-as-pants trend). My waist is smaller than my hips and WAY smaller than my thighs, so I have a tremendous amount of difficulty finding pants that fit. These are so highly rated that I was hopeful. Alas...these are not at all a "curvy" fit, but rather seem to be intended for women whose waist and hip measurements are nearly the same. I'm 5'7" and 138, measurements matching what the size chart listed as a size 9. Though my actual waist is a 28, and the waistline of these pants doesn't hit at my waist, nor is it a 28. I didn't measure it, but you can see how huge it is. The jeans do fit in the butt and thighs, but I could fit my cat in the gap in the waist (I didn't try that, don't worry). I'm giving three stars because they're cute and soft, but the description is inaccurate. They're going back. Also, ladies, if you're shaped like me and can make a recommendation for a brand that will fit, please reply!

Fantastic. PS, real pockets! I lost weight from 178 pounds (80.7 kg) to 149 pounds (67.5 kg). These jeans were my gift to myself as a reward. I'm 5'6" (167.6 cm) tall. I'd have gotten two more, not just one more pair if I could!

I purchased the Galaxy jeans in an 11short. For reference: I am 5’3 and (currently) about 160-165 lbs… uuuggghh… the holidays. Also, my body is mostly an apple type shape. I love these jeans so much! These jeans are a bit loose at the waist which is never the case! LOL! But not loose enough for others to notice - just need to wear a belt with them when I go out. Why so many issues? I am 5’11” and about 165-170lbs with long legs and ordered juniors 13 Long. First pair I ordered 13 long (I have a 35” inseam) that was in May 2022. I loved the fit & they were long enough that I didn’t look like I was waiting for a flood. The seam down the side showed where the inside got pinched with the sewing process but, I was using them for work, so it wasn’t a problem. Second pair I tried on and noticed a worn hole on the butt cheek and some other sewing irregularities. I sent them back and ordered another pair. Third pair came and I tried on and they were a bit tighter and NOT LONGS 😤 they said long but we’re not! I was going to send them back and order another… they are out of my size 13 LONG and now on sale. I’m kinda upset at this since I paid more and they can’t get it right. Or maybe these are all irregulars and others are sending back too? I really tried making this work because I like how these jeans hug around my body not making them feel tight but more like leggings. and now it looks like I’ll be keeping and maybe adding denim to the bottom so I don’t look like pee wee Herman. If the company would like to still correct I’d like them to reach out but, consistency in the jeans is key! I may still return just for the credit because I can’t see spending money on a pair I’m not satisfied with. I hope this helps others.

Wow really Amazon! - updated Great pants, fit really nice. The reason for a 1 star review is because I can't wear them or order a new pair because they are out of stock on my size. No replacement options and it unfortunately took 2 weeks to get these in the first place. Next time look at the clothes you send to customers before you just bag them with a security tag still on them. This is why I don't like ordering clothes online. Too bad, the pants are really cute and fit great. 08/15/20 - I updated this review to 4 stars because it's not the pants fault that Amazon can't seem to pull the security tag off of clothes as stated om many other reviews as well. The pants are great, fit comfortably and in all the right places - my husband was able to get the tag off without putting a hole in the pants like other customers have gotten . So as far as the pants I give 4 stars. We shall see how durable and long they last.

If you got booty...! I am updating my review! I love the color and style of these. They are also very comfortable. I am 5'11", 175-180lbs and got 15longs in Jenna at first and they were too big, then I tried 13long and finally 11long. I have a 29 inch waist, 22 inches per thigh, and about 41 inch hips. I have a booty and struggle so very much to find pants and wear sizes ranging from 8 to 12 depending on the brand and cut. None ever fit though except Silver pants (which are expensive). I carry all my weight in the thighs and hips plus I lift weights, which doesnt help find pants. These fit perfect in the thighs and I loved the length. The waist to hip/thigh ratio was what I really needed to be dramatic and the 11longs came super close. I was nervous the thighs would be too tight but the stretch in them proved how adaptable they are. I have a tad bit of room left in the waistband but its as close as Ive come to perfectly fitting jeans! I definitely reccomend.

Best jeans ever. Amazon, please continue carrying the Juniors version :) Due to a medical issue I gained over 40 lbs and have lost almost all of it. I have been out of jeans for 19 months. My regular weight is 115 and I am now 125 lbs. I bought a 5 as I usually wear a 3. These are so comfortable! I am 48 but have a guys non curvy figure. And I am super out of shape. I think they will conform even when I start to tone up and lose more weight. And I actually think these could work from 115 to 130 and I am 5’4”. Length is good with tennis shoes or heels. Dress up or down. My only problem is I bought a “women’s juniors curvy luscious bootcut. The day AFTER I received my order Amazon stopped carrying the Junior bootcut. I found the same pants at JCPENNEY for 54.00 fee delivery, right now they are on sale for 34.00 plus an extra 20-40 percent off. Oh yes, and the top front is right below your bellybutton and the back top has a nice flattering rise. That means NO MORE plumbers crack, period. It gives your shapes that low rise bootcut look but maintains its placement perfectly! Plus this material launders beautifully, t use cold water and tumble on low. The very best part is they maintain their shape and support comfortably. That means you’ll look just as good walking in your house after work as in the morning. I really hope Amazon restocks the Juniors curvy bootcut. Amazon has separated its stock by size and color but it appears they are going to discontinue this item.

The perfect jean! I’ve been looking for a semi curvy jean with a perfect fit. Most with any stretch show every bump if they’re big enough for my hips and thighs, plus they don’t have a flair/bootcut if they fit. These are loose enough to be comfortable, but appear form fitting with without showing lumps and bumps. I’m 5’11” and the longs are perfect without hanging on the ground or just coming to my ankles. Plus they’re super stylish and cool. My favorite jean in a long time!Update: Blackberry Jean color: I had asked in the questions if this color was black and was told “yes”, but wanted others to know the color is more like blue/black. It’s not solid black, more like a black tinged in blue, charcoal or a very dark midnight blue.

They are flared Lee has a perfect fit for me - Spandex is great and gives a snug fit without cutting off my blood circulation like some jeans do. That being said, these jeans don't fit from the knees down. They flare all right - like jeans I had in the 60s. Which wouldn't be so bad - if they were not so long. See the tag - It says 16 SHORT. I bought these in a "Short" because I wanted to replace a pair of jeans that were too long and frayed and tore at the bottom. These are even worse. In the picture of the jeans on me, I DO have shoes on. Ankles? Yes, those, too. I love LEE, but I wish they really could make a pair of jeans that are short. Not all of us are 6' and over. Not going to return them, but I am disappointed. BTW, they have been washed and did not draw up. But I may try again.

Almost perfect fit, love them I’m 5’9” and ~180lbs. Love the weight of the material, waist height in front, fit throughout the leg, and the diameter of the flare. The waist height in the back is a little bit low and dips a tiny bit too much when sitting. I’m still happy with them and would buy them again

Great for curvy pear/hourglass shaped people! Let me start this by saying I wasn't expecting to fit in these as well as I do. I bought these knowing I could pass them down to my sister if I didn't fit in them as I wanted to. Jeans are hard for me to find that fit well, and I usually opt for trousers or leggings instead. Day 1 of their arrival I wore them to work (see picture) and I had no issues with them. They proved to withstand all the bending over, on my knees and fast movement through an 8 hour shift. They didn't make me uncomfortable, they didn't cause any chafing, or didn't unzip and expose my underwear like I've also experienced with jeans. I got a few compliments from coworkers and customers on them, as they usually dont see me in jeans. The flare on them is adorable, however I will say as im 5'3, I did have to roll them so they didn't drag while at work. So shorties, be aware of the flare drag! Color is beautiful I love the wash. I didn't notice any weird smell from them. And they're very thick, as I stated before I'm constantly on my knees at work and don't feel much stretch from them. I attribute that to their mostly cotton makeup, it's not cheap polyester that easily tears under pressure.

Awesome 1970’s Retro look Returned the bright blue day color, that cost 3 times as much but kept the elevated retro color that was a steal at $16.00 and took a chance on boiling them for 30 minutes on stove top and drying on sanitize cycle for maximum shrinkage. See photo laying on top of retuned pair, the length shrunk almost two inches. Still need to wear with heals, so not for everyday at almost 70 years old, but they make me feel young again. I am 5’3” tall with longer legs and shorter torso. I bought the size 12 short which did shrink all over so if you are big from the knees up these may not be for you. They were scary long looking out of the package but 99% cotton does shrink nicely. If I manage to shrink them a little more they could rate 6 stars!

Check the length before ordering I absolutely love these jeans. I have missed bell bottom jeans and these brought back a lot of memories. I’m size 10/12 jeans, 5 month postpartum and these hold me in all the right places. I’m 5’8 and 177 lbs. The color was exactly like shown in the photos. The only thing is… make sure to check out the length in sizing. I usually wear a regular length but if you look at the length for regular in these jeans it says 33. Longs are 35 and shorts are 31. I should have looked at that more closely and ordered short. That’s my error. The regular were a bit long on me. Next time I will order the short. Lastly, I apologize for the smudgy mirror pics. My littles love to play with that mirror and it always gets smudgy.

Good Flares I was drawn to these jeans because it said they were mid-rise (and on sale). I can’t do high waisted jeans and I have struggled to find mid-rise flares at a reasonable price. After reading the reviews I wasn’t sure if I would like them. Many sizes were sold out because they were on sale. I ordered an 8 regular in a blue wash and an 8 short in a grey wash. As you can see from the pictures, the regular comes up to my belly button. I’m only 5’ 3.5” and weigh about 135 but still. I was disappointed. They were definitely long, but I thought maybe with a heel I could get away with it. Then I tried on the short. They didn’t come up as high (although still a little higher than I wished). And the length was perfect. Some people said their size was a little loose. These are definitely not loose on me. I’m actually hoping they might loosen up a bit after I wear them. I’m returning the 8 regular and plan to keep the 8 short. I haven’t worn them other than to try on so I can’t yet speak for how they will hold their shape after all day. They do have some stretch to them; enough to make them conformable and not stiff.

Good material, odd fit for me. These jeans are quality denim and a great length for taller people. I'm 5'8", 175lbs, and I ordered the "Blue Veil Dx" color option in size 10L. The hem hits the floor and since I usually don't wear flat shoes, this isn't a problem. The price is unbeatable. I haven't owned a pair of Lee jeans in many years. Unfortunately, now I remember why: The fly area always seems to be crooked or slightly off center on Lee jeans. Every pair I've ever tried over the years has had this problem. After wearing for a while, walking around, etc, the fly pulls more to one side and looks crooked. This isn't the case on any other brand of jeans I've tried, so it must be some flaw with this brand. It's weird. But hey, they're nice jeans otherwise, and many people seem to like them. This brand just isn't for me.

Should have read the size chart better If you usually wear Levi's, your usual size will be too skinny and too long here. I wear a size 12 short in Levi's, and they're perfect. These Lee's are too tight in the waist, and ridiculously long. The inseam for a SHORT is 30 inches! I'm 5'4" ish, and I have to stand all the way on my tiptoes to get these to not brush the floor. The waist is about 15.5" across, which is super unflattering and uncomfortable on my post-baby belly. If you are more apple shaped and less hourglass shaped like I am right now, I suggest you size based on the waist measurement, not anything else. That all said, I gave these a 4 because they are excellent quality. They are soft and stretchy, and if it weren't for my own user error and some odd sizing, they would be great!

If you only buy one pair of jeans in your life... THESE are them!!!! Most jeans give me muffin top, sag at the butt, slouch down with my active lifestyle. Eventually I have to wear a belt and my jeans look sad and worn after a few washes. Instantly after putting these on, I just knew. It was heaven! They somehow made my lower stomach/hip area feel snug and controlled, yet No muffin top. I practiced squatting around in them, did a few jumping jacks, etc. There was no shift or need for adjustment. I was amazed. Truly, I did not want to take them off. Felt like second skin but durable. I don't know what kind of magic is in these, but keep it coming! If I wash these a few times and they fall apart, I'll revise my review. But after raving to family about these and those who know about Democracy already, I have a hunch they'll stand the test of time. Okay, they're a little pricy, so start off with a pair that are on a good discount. So far, I can say I'd spend the retail price for the luxury of a good, flattering pant. Photo: I'm 5'5" 140 lbs - size 6 regular

Comfortable waistband but too large for me everywhere else. I was so excited to find Democracy Jeans because I typically have trouble finding jeans that fit my waist comfortably. (I’m 5’7 and 140 pounds. I’d describe my body type as slim but with excess weight in the belly area.) I usually wear a 6, but these jeans were so stretchy that the 6 was too large everywhere so I exchanged for a 4. The 4 fits so nicely at the waist and really hides my stomach! Sitting down is comfortable and I don’t have a bright red waistband line across my stomach when I take these off! But the fit in the legs and butt is too large (definitely doesn’t give me the promised lift) and there are little folds of excess material everywhere. Also, the fly won’t stay up to save my life! Every time I stand up, the barn door is WIDE open. Overall, these jeans deliver comfort… and I love that they exist to accommodate more body types! But I don’t think mine is one of them :(

These are NOT jeggings! These do not fit the same as what is pictured and they are definitely NOT jeggings. You can see how baggy the legs are in the pic I’m 5’7, 145 and these are a size 8, which is my typical size. Fit great in waist and hips but I wanted jeggings. This isn’t it. Disappointed in what seems to be a lot of recent amazon products that aren’t as advertised. Very frustrating.

Nice fit These were recommended to my by a friend who is short and curvy (10S). I got the 6S and am very pleased. I dont have the biggest butt, but being 5'2" and 135, it is difficult to get butt pockets on my actual butt! These are on there. I was reticent regarding the price, so hopefully these hold up for a long time.

Luv this brand Democracy is a great fitting jean have worn for a few years. I usually find them at Marshalls/TJ Maxx for around $30-35. and buy when I can. At $37. these were a great find too. Major storea sell for $60 +. I purchased the Indigo and they are not as seen in the first few photos - more like the last 2 pictures (threading is much lighter not bright gold). I'm 5'6 and 130 and size 4 fits perfectly. I think there is a bit of stretch that will happen while wearing too. If you're looking for a dark denim with comfort give them a try. The elastic in the waist is a bonus. Very similar to the brand JAG - JAG sizes same and material is a bit thicker but has same elastic in waist.

Such comfortable, but trendy jeans !! For us gals who likes a "little stretch" , a great stretchy lower waistband, tummy control, and cute straight legs. Baggy in the knees - not like pic - disappointed I just tried these on and I’m wondering what the raves are about in hiding the lower belly that is stretched from having kids. As I sit, muffin top is real. I’m 5’3 188 pounds so I ordered a 16 short. I received a size 16 but had to roll the pants up to fit my height so didn’t receive short. On the model the pants seem to hug the legs to show great legs but on me the knees are super baggy which is very disappointing. The thigh is baggy, the calf fit is good but these are not short and my knees look like they lost 100 pounds bc the fit is insulting. I utterly hate the process of returns and wish Amazon offered a doorstep return. These cost too much to be disappointed. To avoid the whole return process I’ll just chalk them up to yard pants and take this as this brand isn’t providing the look the model is wearing bc of her knee coverage. I’ve found better on Amazon with pants that are about $20. I’ll take my business there. Not as promised. Any pants I have are a size 16 and have never experienced such a muffin top nor - just picture this - you’re wearing tight leggings but the knee fit is 3xs your knee size.

Relaxed fit, runs a bit loose and long Ordered two pair of these, an 8L (recommended by Amazon) and a 6L (because 6 is my usual size in pants). For reference, I’m 5’7” and 125 lbs, abt a 37” hip. The 8L were big, boxy, too long and too loose (wrong size); the 6L were a better fit—definitely not tight, though at least 4” too long. Returned both these Long-sized Amazon orders, and since Amanda regular 6 was not available, I ordered a pair in that size elsewhere. The reg 6 fits better, though still longer than other regular-sized jeans I own (a plus—can launder without worry). The Amanda style is a more loose-fit style than skinny jeans, and definitely look looser on me than on the model. But they are a higher-waisted, stretchy style, esp good for when you’re going to be doing a lot of sitting (eg. travel). *UPDATE: The 6R seemed to stretch out and get even looser after a few days of travel wearing last week. Based on reviews by others my size or larger, I ordered a size 4R (Zermatt), even though Amazon was now suggesting I order 6L. The 4R is actually the best fit in this Amanda style, and is still plenty long. *PHOTOS are of the 4R. Third time’s a charm! (These pants did need to be washed right away due to strong chemical smell.)

Great jeans... but don't do what I did! lol! Returned another brand to try these. Bought them in white. I'm 5'6.5 and 138 pounds (feel my normal weight should be 130 to 133... I'm working on it, lol!) Anyway, I bought the size 6 short (I'll explain the short part at the end). I could button them, but when you run around in leggings most days, normal pants can feel a little too snug. I thought, "I know! I'll wet them and stretch them over a chair!" No! Bad idea! the next day, they were huge! I wore them and had to pull them up constantly, lol! So I wet them again and put them in the dryer. Fortunately, they tightened up - but that's the point. JUST wear them! Sit in them, walk around. They will loosen up on their own and feel fine! I even thought the thigh felt a little too snug... no... they're fine. Just needed to break them in!The reason I bought short (I normally don't) was just because I heard they ran long... and in the summer, I like to roll up my jeans - especially white ones - just for the look (I like them casual and relaxed) - so I thought if I get short, less rolling and I was right. They're just what I wanted!

True to size After reading a lot of reviews, I took a chance and ordered the pants. I have an apple shape with no butt, so it's hard to find pants that will go around my waist. These fit great! I'm 5'9" and 183 lbs. I bought 18 long and I'm very had happy. If I had a smaller waist, I would have bought smaller size. I like the give in the stretchy fabric. Very comfortable.

Buy these Jeans cause They're Great I bought a size larger than I usually wear because I like wearing roomy jeans. A size 14 would have been more snug and fit perfectly but I like clothes that are comfortably loose. I am 5'7. I immediatly tried them on -- perfect fit for my taste -- then I put them in the washer first and dryer 2nd. They didn't shrink at all! The dryer (with a dryer sheet) actually made them even softer. The color is dark blue and they didn't fade onto the other clothes in the washer at all. A white pillow case came out white! They are comfortable and when I stand up the legs touch my shoe tops. The picture was taken while setting with my legs stretched out.
Fits great, very comfortable I love these jeans. I’ve tried on so many pairs lately and these fit perfectly. I’m 5 foot 10 and weigh 170 pounds and bought an 8 long.

Way better than I had expected Usually I’ll buy some fashion nova jeans in a 15 for $30 and they’ll show up a little tight but stretch out in a few hours. Few months in they’ll tear at the thigh anti-gap lol. A coworker convinced me to get jeans at Target in July. Got 3 pairs of “Levi’s” in a 14 (because they’re stretchy) for like $40/ea. Literally wearing these things to work every day because that’s the only thing I can do through this apocalypse. Some how a pair of these garbage pants ripped in a month. A MONTH. So. I’m on amazon and I see these here bad boys on sale for $9 and I figure I should honestly just buy six pairs and call it a night. Just in case though I upped it to a size 16. If they run small, they’ll fit. If they run regular, I’ll wear a belt. Well guess what. They run regular. The delivery lady Just dropped them off and I pulled one on and these bad boys are so comfortable, but I ordered them big lol. Anyway, I’ll wear a belt to work. I got two pairs but I came back to buy two more, but the sale is over, sad day, but not that sad. They seem way sturdier than the ones on fashion nova AND these pants not only have 4 1/2 Real Pockets but I can also fit my entire hand in the front ones. Heck, I can fit a couple TV remotes in the front pockets. I’m definitely coming back, and even though the $9 sale is over, full price is still a steal compared to Target’s “Levi’s” For reference I’m about 5’5 and 197lbs this week but I’m kindof dense in the bones. A lot of my weight is butt, a lot of it is thighs...these jeans don’t quite do the butt justice but that’s definitely on me because I purposely ordered them too big haha if I figure out how to update this review, I’ll edit to add pictures when I buy the right size

Love Gloria Vanderbilt Amanda jeans , I have worn this brand for years . They fit my body better than any others . I have another patterned jeans from GV and have been searching for another. Finally found it on Amazon got them by using try before you buy. On receiving them, I tried them on immediately sent them to the washing machine turned wrong side out and wash them and dry them. I tried them on again and they were still perfect. No shrinkage immediately notified Amazon and Purchased absolutely love them and get many compliments whenever I wear them I am 5 foot six and 165 pounds I purchased a 12 short and it works for me.

Dont Think This Style's Good for Petites/Shorts, Otherwise Nice Im 5'5" a little over 35.5" in waist, 45" hips, 24" diameter thighs and 16" diameter legs/calves, no thigh gap. What drew me to these pants were that they were high waisted and on a lightning deal for like 20 bucks. I didnt think much about the cut beyond the waist but figured tapered legs would be attractive. I think the deal breaker for me is the style. They look kind of like straight legs on me, and since I have large thighs it's just more fabric than I want in the leg area. I dont like how the fabric bunches behind my knee and though this isn't entirely on the jeans I don't think they're doing my bum any favors. I did go a size larger than i probably should have with the 16, I just hated the idea of the top of the jeans pinching my stomach. I also got the regular instead of the short but I dont think the tapers sharp enough for the extra length to make a difference here, meaning to give shape to my legs. I think the taper's only really attractive if your thighs arent super disproportionate to your legs. Maybe like 2 or 3 inches max wider diameter in the thighs in relation to your calves. At least for the fit I want. I was gonna send these back in exchange for some 14's but I think I'll have to go with one of the other cuts to get the fit I'm looking for. Besides the fit I think these were a good buy for the price. Nice stretch and color. not as thick as some denim blends, a bit thicker than like denim leggings. Comfortable to move around in, liked fit at the waist. Better than most things you'd find between 20 - 30. If I had to recommend based on fabric and comfort vs leg fit I'd recommend.

I dont like how the fabric bunches behind my knee and though this isn't entirely on the jeans I don't think they're doing my bum any favors. I did go a size larger than i probably should have with the 16, I just hated the idea of the top of the jeans pinching my stomach. I also got the regular instead of the short but I dont think the tapers sharp enough for the extra length to make a difference here, meaning to give shape to my legs. I think the taper's only really attractive if your thighs arent super disproportionate to your legs. Maybe like 2 or 3 inches max wider diameter in the thighs in relation to your calves. At least for the fit I want. I was gonna send these back in exchange for some 14's but I think I'll have to go with one of the other cuts to get the fit I'm looking for. Besides the fit I think these were a good buy for the price. Nice stretch and color. not as thick as some denim blends, a bit thicker than like denim leggings. Comfortable to move around in, liked fit at the waist. Better than most things you'd find between 20 - 30. If I had to recommend based on fabric and comfort vs leg fit I'd recommend.

Decreasing quality I love the Amanda jeans and have worn them for years. I bought the lighter Amanda jeans from this listing two years ago, and the darker Portland Wash jeans last week. The finishing on the new pair is so much more raggedy than the older pair after many washings! The fabric content is slightly different: the lighter pair is 73% Cotton, 25% Polyester, 2% Elastane and the darker is 72% Cotton, 26% Polyester, 2% Elastane. The first is made in Jordan, the second in Kenya. I did buy two other pairs in the two year interim, and I returned them as the “fits at natural waist” was uncomfortably well above my belly button. The pairs I kept both needed to be hemmed, but I’m used to that. I’m 5’4” with a 29” inseam. The Short length is too short at 28”, and the Regular length is too long at 30”. This pair of jeans fit comfortably and the darker color is more dressy, which is what I wanted. I’m glad I have a pair I can keep!

OMG...now my favorite jeans! In case you are wondering. I'm 5'9" and about 152 Lbs. I ordered an 8 Long and I could not be any more satisfied with my purchase. Now that I know which size jeans are the most flattering for me, I'm looking forward to ordering more in different colors. I also want to add that there were no design flaws in the material. Now that I'm in Georgia and appreciate some elements of the south, I can't wait to pair these jeans with my cowgirl boots.

Solid pair of pants These are not your "sexy mama date night" jeans, they are however your "mama's got to go to work, move around and be comfortable" jeans. They are made of durable jean material while still feeling stretchy enough to bend, squat, and lift with no problems. The waist band has an added elastic feature so after lunchtime you don't feel like you're going to explode. I'm 5' 1" 130 pounds and got the 8 long. I'm not tall but I find "short" pants to be a little too short for me. These are a little baggy in the leg and extra roomy at the end of the day but honestly I love them. They don't feel like they're going to rip or tear any time soon, not paper thin, and no "decorative" holes. Work appropriate and practical while still being cute. I got the "Seattle" style, not sure what the other styles are like, but definitely interested in trying them out. -a picky pants wearer

They fit! I’ve gain weight over the winter from yet another physical disability and even my “fat jeans” are not as comfortable. Didn’t notice that these were a form of stretch jeans - the flattering fit type . Normally, I shy away from anything ’stretch’ because they simply do not look well on me. As soon as I received them I tried them on to discover that as have to admit that not only do they fit well but the stretchability as not the cheap spandex fabric. It’s tummy smoothing but not to the point of suffocation or back pain or pinching. The fabric is a light to medium firmness around the tummy and derrière and actually comfortable (my problem areas). They’re mid-rise I think and the waist doesn’t dig into my stomach as there’s good stretch there too. Also…no “muffin top”. The legs are long and smoothing also. I would suggest to get your measurements correct to get a good fit. I really like the straight leg silhouette. My legs are more firm and muscular and these jeans don’t squeeze; no struggle getting them on. I have to cold wash them and wear them to see how well they hold up after time. So far I like them. If anything changes I’ll update my review. I bought size 18M, 34 waist, 32 length and I’m about 5’4” and 175 (+/-) lbs.

Soo Comfortable and lookin and feelin fantastic!! I have always bought my pants in a box store. After having four kids, and now going through the beginnings of menopause, buying clothes is not my favorite thing to do. In fact, I downright loathe it. All my jeans have become too tight now, so I’ve been wearing a lot of sweatpants and t-shirts which makes me feel sloppy and like a dirtbag when I’m out and about. When you look good and are dressed decent, you just feel good. I am sooo glad I found these jeans!! I have come to terms that I will never be a size 5 ever again, and so finding the right fit and comfortable jeans have just been almost impossible until now!! These are so dang comfortable, but look good and do a good job being flattering to all my lumps and bumps! I washed these first thing, and they are just so soft and feel beautifully already broke in! I’m 5’5 and these are the perfect length. Not too short not too long. I would call these more straight legged though. Not a super taper which I hate the struggle of taking super tapered pants off. I am just so ecstatic to take a chance on these jeans and now I feel like a million bucks! So much , I bought 3 more pairs!! Update- back to buy 2 more pairs, they are that good! Wash great and just get softer Another update- here to buy some more of these jeans. I’ve read a couple of negative reviews, but for most people they are going to love these jeans. I haven’t found a more comfortable jean that moves with you like these do and fits so well. I’d say for pple 5’3 to 5’5 just order regular sizes, not petite. Anything taller than 5’5 order tall or long.

Big waist gap Size 22 Plus - regular length - Black 5'2" - 235lbs - short torso - large booty I've got what I call a " booty shelf". My bum sticks out a lot, and you could almost set stuff on it. So to be fair, a lot of women my height and weight may not have this issue... The waist gap is big with these jeans. No way can I wear them. When they slide down, which they will inevitably do, they'll still show way too much booty/undies, especially if I bend over and/or my shirt rides up. The regular length is long on me, but that's not unusual. Otherwise the fit seems fine in the hips/thighs. I'm returning them, so of course I don't know how much they'll shrink in the wash. These will probably work great for people without a booty shelf. Since I can't wear them I'm giving 4 stars, but for a different shaped body they could easily be 5 star jeans.

Extremely comfortable but stretchy, stretchy These 22Plus, Gloria Vanderbilt jeans have a huge amount of elastic give which does not allow it be body conforming in the places you want them to be. For example, they do not help you look shapely in the tush, hips and tummy. I am 5'8, 260 pounds with a generous tummy. The jeans do not hide much tummy nor subdue hips and are very loose in the bottom side of the rear, & legs due to the stretch. Without a body shaper on they are a tad loose everywhere. With a shaper on, they are tighter but still comfortable. Yet to me, still not the most flattering look. They fit a tad bit like mom jeans in my opinion. I almost exchanged them for a 20 Plus just to get a more flattering silhouette. But I decided against it as one thing that they definitely are is the most comfortable jeans I ever wore. So, I decided to keep them as I am of the age (56) that comfort trumps style. My first designer jeans at age 14/15 in 1979 were Gloria Vanderbilts (see me in them in 3rd pic) . So buying these were like visiting an old friend that you greatly missed. If nothing else, I am decidely loyal to old friends and our fond memories.

Great but inconsistent/incorrect sizing These fit me great around the waist and hips but the inseam lengths are a mess. For reference I am 5’4”, fluctuate between 135-140lbs and normally take a 6/28 (or a 4/27 in Madewell). My inseam from inner thigh to floor is exactly 30”. I ordered the 6/28 short and they said 28x30” on the jeans when they arrived but they were short (picture with the white sneaker and leopard loafers) so I measured and it wasn’t even 29” inseam. I sent them back and got 6/28 regular length and that is supposed to be a 32” inseam (which would still be too long on me) but they were closer to 31” inseam (picture with the cat). I am keeping them and may have them hemmed but with shoes the length seems ok. They fit great in the hard to fit areas with a good amount of stretch but something is fishy with the inseams.

Beyoncé influenced me… Thanks to Beyoncé and her new team up with Levi’s, I decided to try this brand. Yes, I was influenced. I usually wear a size US 16/L in bottoms and bought these in a size 16. They almost fit. The thigh and leg area are nice, could stand to be a bit looser for my taste but not a deal breaker. What IS a deal breaker is that the band around the waist makes these feel EXTREMELY tight. I should have sized up to an 18. I’m iffy about whether I want to give it a try in an 18 or not. I might cave and give it a try. The length was perfect. I’m 5’7” and the regular length was fine. Probably helped that they’re bootcut. The material felt so soft. Like a jean/ legging hybrid. I’m too lazy to send them back but these will make a great Christmas present for a family member. These would be a great buy if you are able to pick your correct size.

Good quality, comfortable, great price! These pull-on bootcut jeans are fantastic quality for the price. The fabric is substantial like regular jeans, but soft and stretchy. I love that these don't have a zipper and button to cut into my belly = very comfortable! I don't have much in the way of curves, and I do think that they would look better on you than on me if you have some. I'm 5'2 and 110lb, and the size 2 is just a little big on me (the 29" length is perfect), so I wish they came in my usual size zero, but they are so comfy and soft I'm keeping them. I guessing that these in size 2/short would work well for ~120lb, 130 if you like your jeans tight.

Not for tall girls Bummed these didn't work. I am 5'10", 170lbs and ordered the 10 Long. They are way too short for my taste. I'm fine with ankle length with a skinny pair of jeans but expect my boot/flair to at least touch the ground barefoot or with flats. I couldn't find the inseam length in the description when ordering but it turns out they are 32". I'm accustomed to "Regular" length being 32", "Long" at 34". Quality is really nice. I was worried that since they're pull-ons, that they would be be too stretchy or thin. They are 68% cotton, so that helps deliver the weight I like. As I am returning them, I will never know if they get majorly loose or saggy in the crotch after wear/ washing. LOVE that all the pockets are functional. Love the dark wash. They are form fitting in the bottom, thighs and waist. No gaping at the back. Waist lays right at the bellybutton. Wish there were belt loops in case you wear a shirt that reveals the top of the pants. I love wearing pull on jeans but I don't want anyone to know they are! Again, I am sad they aren't a true "Long" otherwise I'd give them 5 stars.

I'm 5'3" 115-120 lbs, I usually wear size Small/4. I ordered these jeans in my usual size 4, and they fit true to size. The jeans have spandex in them, so they're very stretchy - I can high-kick, squat, and do the splits in these jeans. They pull up like leggings, there's no zipper or buttons, they just pull right on. The pockets are functional. I usually wear a 28" inseam snd I ordered these in a 30" accidentally so I'll have to hem them, but these jeans are otherwise perfect, they look like normal jeans, but they're so stretchy and comfy that they feel like wearing leggings. The waistband pulls up over my love handles, eliminating muffintop. My new favorite jeans!

I bought these because I would be traveling in a car for several days and wanted to be comfortable. I was very comfortable. I wore them for 4 straight days. I know that's a long time, but no one pointed to me and said " lady do you ever change your pants?". Very comfy walking on the Appalachian trail ( gotta brag as I'm 62yrs. young). As you see in photo the thread is a little loose. I'll just put a drop off clear nail polish or frey check on it for now instead of returning them. I did wash them when I got home inside out and air dried. They look fine. Size was good. I'm five' 5" feet and 130 pounds. I wore them with sandles as I was traveling from FL. to CO. Savannah, GE was wonderful!! If you go stay on the river in the Marriott with the balcony! Any way, these jeggings were great for travel. My daughter said they are good for old people like me. I took her out of my Will. Try these, you won't be disappointed. Update! These jeggings hold up to falls!! I was running on loose rocks when my daughter's dog ( who is no longer in my Will ) cut me off and I fell. I was wearing my jeggings at the time. I landed on one knee and wrist very hard while landing on my side. My knee did not tear the jegging material yet my knee was cut and bruised. I fell hard, broke three toes, and injured my shoulder, but the jeggings are fine! There is just a little place where my knee hit the rocks that made the fabric a little lighter blue but not noticeable to not wear it again. I'm wearing an orthopedic boot for my broken toes for a few months, but I think the jeggings will look cute with the boot when I go to Maine to hike a little bit on the Appalachian Trail. I have good insurance. So again, I would recommend these, although I wouldn't recommend running on rocks with a large dog thinking it's playtime. And I question why would a husband ask "Are you O.K." when finding you bleeding and laying down on rocks?

Runs a little small, but great jeggings 2023 UPDATE - when I say that I made these nearly an everyday staple for the past 2 years, I'm not joking. I wore these almost everyday, washed and dried them in the dryer endlessly. I finally purchased another pair (identical size and color as previously) and I am AMAZED at how well these held up! The wash slightly faded and there is one small spot where a thread loop (decorative only) came out of one back pocket.. that's IT. The fit hasn't shrunk in the slightest. So DO NOT HESITATE to buy these extremely high quality for the price and comfy jeggings!!! I'm always looking for inexpensive comfy jeggings for around the house when I want to feel like I'm still in pajamas but not necessarily *look* like I'm wearing pajamas. Grabbed these in a size Small. FIT I would say these run small. Not insanely small, but in particular the elastic waistband is too small to be comfortable. I'm a 28" waist and generally order everything in XS but this waistband is really tight on my waist. Other than the waistband I like the fit of these leggings. I am 5'3 and they hit just below my ankle which I prefer. QUALITY These are super stretchy but still look like authentic Jean fabric and stitching so it looks good and feels easy to move around in. LOOK/STYLE I like the wash of these jeggings, they're exactly as I hoped for. I don't think that the overly small waistband makes these flattering on me so I will only be wearing these with longer shirts that cover most of that area. The stitching and look of the fabric is an authentic Jean look and not cheap looking at all. FINAL THOUGHTS I do think these run small and if you dry clothes in the dryer like I do, I expect these will eventually shrink and become even smaller. I recommend sizing up at least one size, maybe 2 if you really want to be comfy. I would likely purchase more of these in more colors and medium size next time

This is the first pair of jeggings I've purchased, and I LOVE them!!!!!! They look and feel like real denim jeans! I'm not a fan of 'skinny' jeans, but I decided to give them a go (because I can't find anything similar in boot cut or flare without a hefty price tag). I've seen pairs of jeggings at Walmart that looked.. well... not good lol. I've also seen other people wearing jeggings and you can totally tell that they are, in fact, jeggings. They look kinda cheap? But not these, they look amazing. I've never felt more confident in any other pair of pants (is it technically 'bottoms'?). I'm very happy with my purchase. I will give you a break down of my body shape and weight. Something that I absolutely hate!!! I have fluctuated up in down weight wise my whole life. I have some extra skin as a reminder. But, here goes... I am roughly 5'8" and weigh 240. I am sort of awkwardly proportioned. According to me lol. I carry most of my fat in my belly. I have a hard time finding real jeans that actually fit me properly, because my legs aren't that big. My natural waist is 44, my hips are 54, and my lower hip measurement is 34. So when I try on jeans in a size I can get up high enough with my tum tum, the legs always look baggy and weird. Anyway, I think it's important to remember that I could be standing next to someone that is the same height and weight, and we will not necessarily be the same size. My weight is in my belly, but some are fortunate enough to have there's evenly distributed. I ordered an XL, because I fit in to a XL in yoga pants, and leggings. They fit me perfectly. The length, feel, and fit was amazing. They aren't as thin as leggings, but they aren't as thick as some denim jeans. They structure to them, so they don't highlight every bulge. I hope this helps someone.

Love them!! I love these pants! At 5’5” and 220 it was hard to find cute jeans/pants in the “skinny” style. I needed something much more fitted at the ankle than my big girl jeans! Thought I’d give these a try and I’m glad I did! They are thick enough they don’t look like leggings, just nice enough to wear to work, and stretchy enough to be comfortable! I have now lost 30 lbs and I’m still wearing them! No need to buy every size as I’m losing weight. XL fit then and fits now. I might not have to buy again until I get into a medium!! Those reviewers whose pants split right away were likely wearing them waaay too small. If you’re on the fence, go for it!

Not For My Body Type I thought because these were leggings they would fit tighter in the thighs, hips, behind & ankles. I also thought they would be longer in length & rise. But they are much too large, in those areas & too short for my body type. Because the rise is too short, they fit below the belly button, unless you want to pull them up & have a camel toe. As a point of reference, I’m more on the tall & thin side at 5’9”, 128lbs, 28” waist, 36” hips with thin thighs & not much behind. The fabric is soft & comfortable, but just not a good fit for me. They were so inexpensive that it doesn’t make sense to send them back. So I will donate them instead.

Had several tiny holes- fabric too thin They came with several tiny holes in fabric. I will say this fabric is extremely thin. I tried them on after noticing the holes in order to determine if I should replace or outright return them. They fit really well, true to size (I'm 5'8, 250 & bought 2x), however given the type of fabric - which was Not really denim, but rather somewhere between a fabric stretch jegging and tights - it seemed that if they hadn't already been damaged upon arrival, they wouldn't have lasted more than a couple a couple wears without damage anyway. Opted for the refund. If these had been a slightly more durable or even reasonably decent fabric, I would've kept them because the fit seemed nice.

Inconsistent sizing at its finest! I absolutely loved these jeans! I’ve had over a dozen pair. I needed a few new ones and really liked the new color, “Best Seller”. I ordered 2 at my normal size, 30. I’m 5’4”, approx 165lbs. 1st pair I tried on, I couldn’t get over my thighs. I’m a powerlifter, so I have THICC thighs. I figured my old ones must just be really stretched out. NOPE! The second pair I got up, barely, but couldn’t even close them to button! There’s like a 3” gap! My normal pair, I have about 3” too much. The first photo is the second pair I tried, the last 2 photos are my original pair, in a different color. Both pairs are the same size. I’ll most likely need to go up to, at least a 34. Very unfortunate. Although, returns are super easy, it’s still inconvenient!

They stretch quickly and material is low quality Original review: I liked these when I got them because they're FINALLY a decent length for me. I'm 5'6" and regular length jeans typically have a 29" inseam which is just too short. These have a 30" inseam which is perfect. When I purchased, I bought a 34 and a 33 and the 33 fit perfect. They're reasonably soft while also being a decent weight. However, after wearing just a short while, they're immediately sagging and stretched just a little too much. If I was to buy again, I'd size down to a 32. More disappointing than the sizing though which is just a minor thing is that I've worn them about two weeks and I already have two holes/pulls on the butt. I have worn jeans for 30+ years and never, ever had a pair develop holes like this. For material that I thought was a heavy and good quality, I'm surprised they got damaged so easily. Edited 12/20/23 - more pulls/holes in the butt. See pic I added. I wear jeans literally every day and have never had this happen to a single other pair of jeans I've owned. I have to assume this is a problem specifically with Levi or this batch or something but I will not be ordering another pair. They also shrunk (shortened) after a few washes. I'm moving on and will look elsewhere for my next pair.
